https://www.adzuna.be/details/5614685520.scraped: Volvo Group Operations Graduate Program – Traineeship Over 15 exciting months, the operations graduate program will give you a comprehensive view of the Volvo Group’s global truck manufacturing organization. You’ll explore our brands, plants, logistics, and key business areas like manufacturing, quality, and continuous improvement. You'll also engage with emerging technologies and develop future-ready skills. From day one of the traineeship, you’ll hold a permanent position, supported by a manager and mentor, with opportunities for “on-the-job” rotations and real project experience. Through team development sessions and learning modules, you'll connect with other participants and grow into your future career at Volvo. If you're seeking a traineeship with global exposure and the chance to shape the future, this role could be your next step. Our organization You will be part of our cab over trucks organization. The Ghent factory is the largest truck factory of the Volvo Group worldwide. Every day, nearly 3000 colleagues assemble high quality trucks. Do you believe that motivated employees are the result of strong leadership? Are you an inspiring communicator who knows how to guide people on their path to personal growth? Find out what the operations graduate program could offer you! Your role as an OGP Future Leader at Volvo Group As Future Leader, you are responsible for guiding and motivating your team to achieve the production goals together. You develop your employees into professionals who can work efficiently in a dynamic environment where change is the standard. Thanks to your personal approach, they receive the individual coaching they need to get the best out of themselves. You find the right balance between empathy, autonomy and hierarchy. You ensure that your team strictly follows the rules regarding safety, quality, ergonomics and the environment, and encourage them to both respect procedures and make suggestions for improvement. Are you our Future Leader? Leadership comes naturally to you. You know how to create consensus between different groups. You are self-aware, know how to motivate others and correct them when necessary. You think in terms of “we”: moving forward together, growing together. You offer clear guidance, clear rules and room for personal development. Planning, organizing and optimizing are second nature to you. Continuous improvement is your motivation. You combine a broad overview of the organization with attention to detail and strive for the best results for each KPI. Your enthusiasm is contagious and motivates your team to improve, innovate and take on challenges. You hold (or will graduate with) a bachelor's or master's degree and have a maximum of two years of previous full-time working experience, prior or after graduation. You have an excellent command of both Dutch and English. Are we a perfect match? Do you recognize yourself in this profile? Apply now via our website! Want to hear about the program from our current OGP's? Check it out here! Please send us your application before the 11th of March 2026. We will not accept new applications after this. Start of the traineeship will be mid August 2026. Volvo Group is one of the leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, earth-moving machinery, engines and powertrains for recreational and commercial vessels, as well as industrial applications. The Ghent facility is the Volvo Group's largest truck factory in the world. The Ghent site is also home to several logistics activities, including the group's largest distribution center. In addition, you will also find a number of European services such as assistance and breakdown assistance for drivers (Volvo Action Service) and technical support for dealers (Technical Dealer Support). As a newcomer at Volvo Group, you’ll have the opportunity to work in a dynamic, international environment where you’ll apply the latest technologies in the transport and logistics sector. You will have the chance to grow, use your talents, and shape your future in the sector. And you can try our famous Swedish meatballs in the cafeteria! Check out our vacancies!

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5614685516.scraped: Volvo Group Operations Graduate Program - Traineeship Over 15 exciting months, the operations graduate program will give you a comprehensive view of the Volvo Group’s global truck manufacturing organization. You’ll explore our brands, plants, logistics, and key business areas like manufacturing, quality, and continuous improvement. You'll also engage with emerging technologies and develop future-ready skills. From day one of the traineeship, you’ll hold a permanent position, supported by a manager and mentor, with opportunities for “on-the-job” rotations and real project experience. Through team development sessions and learning modules, you'll connect with other participants and grow into your future career at Volvo. If you're seeking a traineeship with global exposure and the chance to shape the future, this role could be your next step. What will you do? To reach the next level within our Production Control Program & Planning Department and our Logistics organization, we want to get a better grip on all the information we have in our systems. As a Digital Innovation Analyst Trainee, you will play a pivotal role in supporting logistics operations within the Volvo Group. You will work across end-to-end (E2E) processes, leveraging data-driven insights to analyze, optimize, and streamline workflows across production, supply chain, and logistics. Your future team You will join a dynamic, collaborative team within the Volvo Group focused on logistics and planning. Our team consists of experienced planners and a business analyst from diverse backgrounds, working together to deliver efficient supply chain solutions. We foster a culture of learning, innovation, and teamwork, encouraging initiative, creativity, and continuous improvement. Who are you? Do you enjoy turning data into actionable insights and using analytics to improve planning and logistics performance? Are you passionate about working with diverse teams to identify opportunities and implement best practices? Would you like to contribute to projects that optimize supply chains and improve production planning and inventory management? Are you excited about driving digitalization and automation initiatives to make processes smarter and more efficient? Do you have a knack for creating clear reports and dashboards that help stakeholders make informed decisions? Are you eager to learn and adapt quickly to new IT systems and tools in a dynamic logistics environment? Grab the chance to apply all your theoretical and practical knowledge from school at a world-class leader in the automotive industry! Required knowledge/experience Master’s degree in engineering, IT, Supply Chain or a related field, with an interest in digitalization and data driven environments. Maximum two years of previous working experience (prior to or after graduation). Good understanding of production planning, logistics, and supply chain principles Analytical mindset, able to read data and translate it into tangible advice & insights. In addition, you have the learning ability to quickly work with IT systems and Tools (e.g. Excel, BI Tools, ...) Strong communication skills, with the ability to work comfortably in diverse, cross-functional teams while acting with integrity, credibility, and consistency. You possess strong problem-solving skills and can turn complex challenges into practical solutions You critically analyze situations and take the initiative to tackle bottlenecks efficiently. As this is a truly global program, you need to be fluent in both speaking and writing in English. A very good knowledge of Dutch is mandatory. What’s in it for you? 15-month trainee program during which you will be working on current, business-critical projects with integrated local and international job rotations You are given your own tasks and responsibilities from the start Benefit from personal career and team development events Alongside other program participants, you will attend a variety of workshops and seminars designed to help you grow into your future career at Volvo Group Receive a competitive salary and permanent position from day one Benefit from individual support, coaching and feedback from your mentor and experts within your field Build a network within the organisation to foster your career development Take the driver’s seat by shaping the program according to your own personal interests to develop your competencies for tomorrow In addition, you will take a deep dive into some specific areas of our business such as manufacturing, health and safety, quality, and continuous improvements, while also learning about the end-to-end supply chain, aftermarket parts and our remanufacturing operations. We furthermore offer the opportunity for you to be part of our digital transformation, applying new technologies to connect and automate our processes. Together we work with AI, data science, machine learning, circular systems, virtual manufacturing and more, to build our fully electric or autonomous products. If rotational development, a global set up and designing the world of tomorrow are aspects you are looking for in your next job context, this position might be something for you. Are we a perfect match? Are you a true data wizard, keen to understand what is underneath the surface? Are you a real team player and do you want to work in dynamic changing environment? Then you are who we are looking for! Do not hesitate and apply now! Please send us your application before the 11th of March 2026. We will not accept new applications after this. Start of the traineeship will be mid August 2026. Volvo Group is one of the leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, earth-moving machinery, engines and powertrains for recreational and commercial vessels, as well as industrial applications. The Ghent facility is the Volvo Group's largest truck factory in the world. The Ghent site is also home to several logistics activities, including the group's largest distribution center. In addition, you will also find a number of European services such as assistance and breakdown assistance for drivers (Volvo Action Service) and technical support for dealers (Technical Dealer Support). As a newcomer at Volvo Group, you’ll have the opportunity to work in a dynamic, international environment where you’ll apply the latest technologies in the transport and logistics sector. You will have the chance to grow, use your talents, and shape your future in the sector. And you can try our famous Swedish meatballs in the cafeteria! Check out our vacancies!

Develop and validate predictive models, dashboards, and reports to monitor business performance. Ensure data quality, governance, and compliance with Volvo Group standards. Support the implementation of AI-driven initiatives, such as process automation, demand forecasting, and customer analytics. Build understanding of the business/operations and business challenges in our operations Come up with improvement suggestions leveraging AI/ML/Data. Who are you? You are curious by nature and passionate about using data to solve real business problems. With a background in analytics, engineering, or data science, you know how to turn raw information into meaningful insights, whether through visualization tools or coding in SQL and Python. More importantly, you can explain those insights in a simple, compelling way that helps others act with confidence. You thrive in diverse, international teams and enjoy learning from different perspectives. Change doesn’t unsettle you, it energizes you, because you see it as a chance to adapt and grow. What defines you most is your mindset: collaborative, open, and motivated by the belief that data and AI can create real, lasting impact for Volvo Group and the world around us. To qualify for our operations graduate program you have a maximum of two years of previous full time working experience, prior to or after graduation. As this is a truly global program, you need to be fluent in both speaking and writing in English. Ready for the next move? If you're seeking global exposure and the chance to shape the future, this role could be your next step. Curious? Apply now! Please send us your application before the 11th of March 2026. We will not accept new applications after this. Start of the traineeship will be mid August 2026. Volvo Group is one of the leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, earth-moving machinery, engines and powertrains for recreational and commercial vessels, as well as industrial applications. The Ghent facility is the Volvo Group's largest truck factory in the world. The Ghent site is also home to several logistics activities, including the group's largest distribution center. In addition, you will also find a number of European services such as assistance and breakdown assistance for drivers (Volvo Action Service) and technical support for dealers (Technical Dealer Support). As a newcomer at Volvo Group, you’ll have the opportunity to work in a dynamic, international environment where you’ll apply the latest technologies in the transport and logistics sector. You will have the chance to grow, use your talents, and shape your future in the sector. And you can try our famous Swedish meatballs in the cafeteria! Check out our vacancies!

Contribute to day-to-day activities within the department Being part of an experienced team of marketers & salespeople You will be granted a high level of individual ownership and end responsibility in managing the assigned tasks What makes you a good fit Academic degree in Business/Economic studies with strong interest in Marketing and (online) media Enthusiastic, creative person who thinks in terms of solutions Analytical skills and a good sense for facts and figures Very good knowledge of the MS Office Package (especially MS Excel & Powerpoint) Excellent knowledge of English and French Available to start in July 2026 for a period of 6 months Some perks of joining Henkel An exciting and educational full-time opportunity in an international FMCG organization The possibility to support concrete and business-relevant projects with a lot of responsibilities and development opportunities Be part of a fun and dynamic team, with events and activities being organized on a regular basis A competitive internship allowance 10 days of holidays Hybrid working environment: 60% office, 40% home office. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5628205478.scraped: As part of the expansion of our activities in the process industries, we are looking for a junior Project / Process Engineer to join our teams in the Ghent region. You will have the opportunity to work on industrial projects within sectors such as food & beverage , pharmaceuticals , chemicals , biotech , and heavy industry , supporting our clients in the transformation, optimization, and reliability of their production facilities. This role is ideal for a young graduate eager to develop strong technical and project‑management skills in a fast‑moving industrial environment. ABOUT THE POSITION: As a Project / Process Engineer, you will take part in various engineering activities depending on project needs and client expectations. Your responsibilities may include: Supporting process and project engineering studies (design, calculations, mass & energy balances, equipment sizing…). Participating in layout design, P&IDs, process documentation, and technical specifications for equipment or systems. Assisting in the follow‑up of suppliers, contractors, and fabrication activities. Contributing to project coordination tasks: planning, risk analysis, quality follow‑up, and reporting. Participating in site visits, surveys, construction supervision, commissioning preparation, or FAT/SAT activities. Collaborating closely with multidisciplinary teams (mechanical, automation, utilities, quality, HSE…). As you grow in experience, you will progressively gain autonomy and take on broader responsibilities within projects. ABOUT YOU: Master’s degree in Industrial, Electromechanics, Mechanical or Bioscience Engineering Interest in both technical content and project coordination. Fluent in Dutch and English (French can be an additional asset for specific projects) Available to start in September/October 2026 ABOUT US: Vulcain Engineering is a group specialized in the energy sector that supports companies active in Renewable & Nuclear Energy, Gas & Infrastructure through our Study Offices and Consultancy services. Vulcain Engineering Group is positioned as a transformation partner thanks to its ultra-specialization in strategic sectors which positively impact the transformation of our society and supports its clients in their transformation towards more sustainable and responsible models. Vulcain supports its clients throughout the entire life cycle of their projects, from design to commissioning, by integrating constructability and safety requirements right from the design phase. In Belgium, our activities focus on consultancy services, delivering expertise in three main areas: Energy/Infrastructure Gas, Renewables & Processes Life Sciences With headquarters based in Brussels, and clients spread all over the country, we address key projects in the Belgian industry, offering our consultants the opportunity to work on challenging topics and discover different areas during their time at Vulcain. Why would you apply? An annual gross salary ranging from 37 000€ to 39 000€ paid over 13,92 months and advantages such as a company car (and fuel card), representation fees, insurances, meal vouchers, eco cheques, a sectorial bonus (the salary indicated corresponds to the level of experience mentioned in the job description. For any other level of experience, the annual salary may vary accordingly) A tailored career path within the company : opportunities to discover different business units, training, follow-up from your business manager, and a development evaluation every year A human-based company with slightly more than 120 consultants and synergies between the different business units

Vulcain supports its clients throughout the entire life cycle of their projects, from design to commissioning, by integrating constructability and safety requirements right from the design phase. In Belgium, our activities focus on consultancy services, delivering expertise in three main areas: Energy/Infrastructure Gas, Renewables & Processes Life Sciences With headquarters based in Brussels, and clients spread all over the country, we address key projects in the Belgian industry, offering our consultants the opportunity to work on challenging topics and discover different areas during their time at Vulcain. Why would you apply? An annual gross salary ranging from 37 000€ to 39 000€ paid over 13,92 months and advantages such as a company car (and fuel card), representation fees, insurances, meal vouchers, eco cheques, a sectorial bonus (the salary indicated corresponds to the level of experience mentioned in the job description. For any other level of experience, the annual salary may vary accordingly) A tailored career path within the company : opportunities to discover different business units, training, follow-up from your business manager, and a development evaluation every year A human-based company with slightly more than 120 consultants and synergies between the different business units

The Oncidium foundation is a Belgian non-profit and public-benefit organization that aims to support and accelerate the development of radiotheranostics for cancer care with the objective of improving patient access worldwide. Radiotheranostics represent an innovative technology in nuclear medicine, using both a diagnostic and therapy tool, to detect and treat the same disease. Administered orally or by injection, radiotheranostics could represent a viable option and potentially be lifesaving and enhancing for people living with cancer. There is still a lot to be done to raise awareness among doctors, oncologists, patients and the general public about the functioning and benefits of this new diagnostic/therapeutic approach and thus, to enhance availability globally.

A place where learning speed matters more than your CV Key message for candidates This is not about ticking tool boxes. It’s about curiosity, ownership, and wanting to understand how things really work.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5628203284.scraped: Job Title: IT Analyst Language: English, Dutch Contract Duration: 02/03/2026 - 31/12/2026 Location: Lodderstraat 16, 2880 Bornem, Belgium Work mode: Hybrid (2 or 3 day onsite per week) Work regime: Full time Job Description - Roles and Responsibilities We are looking for a driven IT Analyst who enjoys analyzing, designing, and developing systems within an Agile/DevOps environment. As a full-fledged member of a multidisciplinary feature team, you will work on innovative solutions, translate business needs into clear technical requirements, and contribute to a genuine DevOps culture by also taking on Operations tasks. Leadership in agile ceremonies and fostering innovation within networks are central to this role. You will deliver analysis and implementation in a local team that provides services and applications for CLIENT Financial Services Belux, focusing on fast value delivery and high-quality output in the local market. At the same time, you act as a bridge between the business (Belux) and the central CLIENT applications and services: translating business needs into concrete technical requirements and ensuring seamless alignment between the local context and central infrastructure. You maintain a holistic view of both local and central systems so that changes, integrations, and optimizations deliver value for both Belux and the broader CLIENT ecosystem. Key Responsibilities Analyze, design, develop, and test systems for assigned products/applications. Drive technical innovation and evangelize new technologies within formal and informal networks. Expand responsibilities to Operations to embed DevOps practices. Be a full member of an Agile feature team; support and/or lead agile ceremonies and refine business and technical requirements. Translate business needs into clear technical and functional specifications and collaboratively refine them with the team. Collaborate with business and IT stakeholders to ensure value, quality, and speed. Maintain a simplified, result-oriented mindset with a pragmatic approach and attention to detail. Required Technology Knowledge (Medior Level) Solid understanding of software development life cycle (SDLC) and Agile/DevOps methodologies. Experience with system analysis and design tools such as UML, BPMN, or similar. Proficiency in at least one programming or scripting language (e.g., Java, C#, Python, JavaScript). Familiarity with relational databases and SQL. Knowledge of API design and integration (RESTful services, SOAP). Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud) is a plus. Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and tools (e.g., Jenkins, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps). Basic knowledge of containerization and orchestration technologies (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) is advantageous. Familiarity with monitoring and logging tools used in Operations (e.g., Prometheus, ELK stack) is a plus. Good grasp of version control systems (e.g., Git). Required skills Dutch and English expericene in confluence and Jira experience of +3 years with analyse methodologies knowledge of API design and integrations Skills & Requirements Analytical methodologies (UML, BPMN, …) 3 Years – Medior API design and integrations 3 Years – Medior Automotive sector 4 Years – Minimum 2 Years Confluence 3 Years – Medior Dutch Yes English Yes Financial sector 4 Years – Minimum 2 Years Jira – Kanban & Scrum 3 Years – Medior

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5581422165.scraped: Line of Service Advisory Industry/Sector Not Applicable Specialism Deals Management Level Intern/Trainee Job Description & Summary Leading with heart, living by values PwC Advisory is a garden of possibilities, and you’re reaping the rewards. Where you want to make an impact is always up to you. With the full support of our Advisory teams, you’ll delve into the inner workings of companies to find the best solutions for any challenge they might have. From the excitement of deals to the complexities of consulting and the fascinating realm of risks and threats in forensics, you’ll help clients nurture trust and maintain their evolutionary edge, today and into the future. Now that’s making a difference. We’re looking for talented interns to join our Deals Analytics team for 2026-2027. Are you fascinated by Mergers & Acquisitions as well as the technological side of this business? Would you like to work on various transactions and turn business data into meaningful intelligence for sellers and candidate investors? PwC’s Deal Analytics team are bringing both together. In today’s world, data is becoming one of the key sources for value creation. Yet, companies are struggling to make data have a direct impact in their projects and on their business. The fast paced M&A environment is no exception to that. We are making it happen. As an intern, you will be involved in a wide variety of tasks: What you’ll learn and do: Being part of the project team on live Deals; Using data manipulation on different raw source files to make a first analysis possible; Automate manual processes and set up reusable workflows to improve efficiency and effectivity; Linking and visualizing datasets into informative and attractive dashboards; Support clients in analysis of key business drivers; Play around in tools like Alteryx, PowerBI and Tableau as well as do some coding in R, Python; Explore new solutions and ideas for advanced analysis.​ Who you are: A final year Master student in an technology-related programmes or study informatics, civil or business engineering (policy informatics), mathematics, (applied) economics or business studies. Specialise, or have a particular interest, in data analytics/data science and are interested in business management Are sociable and communicative. In addition to having an analytical spirit, you also are curious and have a stiff dose of commercial flair. Are fluent in English & Dutch and/or French Have a school contract What we offer: The chance to work closely with different teams within our Deals department (Due Diligence, Corporate Finance, Valuation…) The opportunity to learn more about the practical use of data and the exciting projects we deal with on a daily basis. A daily allowance and transportation reimbursement. To be part of a company with an international network of expert teams seeking the best solutions for national and international clients. Testimonial: “An internship with the Deals analytics team is a great place to grow” Being part of the deals team was a great opportunity to learn, use my theoretical knowledge as well as bring my skills to the next level. The team is very supportive and responsive making homeworking a better experience. I’ve been assigned to diverse types of projects; from financial and geospatial analysis, to machine learning and computer vision. The tasks keep changing which creates more challenges and learning opportunities for the trainees. Omar - Intern in Deals Analytics During the time of your internship, you will have the chance to work closely together with different teams within our Deals department (Due Diligence, Corporate Finance, Valuation …). This will give you the opportunity to learn more about the practical use of data as well as the different exciting projects we are dealing with on a daily basis. Looking for a career where you’ll really make a difference? Then we look forward to meeting you! https://www.adzuna.be/details/5623377380.scraped: The fundamental role of an auditor is to report to the shareholders as to whether the company's annual accounts show a true and fair view of the company's financial activity. As well as to ensure that the accounts are presented in conformity with the prevailing legal and regulatory requirements. An auditor at KPMG, you will be doing much more than just looking at the company's financial records. You'll be finding out as much as you can about the whole company, the way it works, its products or services, its technology, its competitors and the industry in which it operates. As an audit-trainee you'll be working within an audit team, where you'll assist in the audit of the annual account of several clients. This includes amongst other things: Updating the audit plan and the risk-analysis. Following-up the confirmation procedure. Verifying different sections of the cash balances and financial statements by using audit tools. Monitoring the administrative organization and the internal control. You'll be working both at the client and at the office. Where? Liège. Louvain-La-Neuve. You will soon hold a Master degree in Management, Commercial or Business Engineering, Commercial Sciences or (Applied) Economic Sciences. A background in Accountancy is an asset. We’re looking for students with excellent proficiency in French , and a good command of English. While we believe academic qualifications are an important pointer as to how you might succeed at our firm, these are far from being the only thing you'll be assessed on. There are several other capabilities we believe you'll need to demonstrate while you're with KPMG: Client centricity. Team work. Problem solving skills. Planning and organizing skills. Communication skills. At KPMG , we truly believe our people are the heartbeat to our success. We commit ourselves to encouraging true team spirit and creating an international and dynamic working environment in which each can pursue the knowledge needed to boost their career. Additionally, as Top Employer, we highly invest in our people’s development of soft skills and technical know-how. KPMG offers of the state-of-the-art tools , solutions and support (IT, library, and databases) to allow you to concentrate on the work at hand and maximize your efficiency. The firm invests heavily in its people in terms of training , welfare , incentive and reward , and prides itself on maintaining a friendly , informal and sociable working atmosphere. In een wereld die voortdurend in beweging is en steeds complexer wordt, helpt KPMG bedrijven en organisaties om zich succesvol aan te passen aan die razendsnel veranderende omstandigheden. Om risico’s en bedreigingen om te buigen in kansen. Onze audit-, fiscale- en adviesdiensten helpen ondernemingen om te groeien, om de risico’s efficiënt te beheren en om betere resultaten te halen. We streven ernaar om duurzame relaties op te bouwen met onze klanten, en dan is het cruciaal om volgehouden kwaliteit te blijven garanderen. Geen hokjesdenken bij KPMG. Geen twee bedrijven of organisaties zijn gelijk, multinationals hebben andere vragen en andere noden dan KMO’s. KPMG zorgt voor een aanpak op maat van elke individuele klant en elke specifieke uitdaging. Onze experts werken samen in multidisciplinaire teams, waar we alle specialisaties en expertise bundelen die nodig is om elke opdracht tot een goed einde te brengen. We staan dicht bij onze klanten en begeleiden hen bij elke stap van hun transformatieproces. Van de prille probleemanalyse tot het monitoren en bijstellen van de oplossingen die we uitwerken. KPMG België maakt deel uit van een groot internationaal netwerk. KPMG is actief in 156 landen, met meer dan 152.000 medewerkers. Die internationale kijk laat ons toe om nieuwe trends en evoluties snel op te pikken, en al even snel antwoorden te formuleren op die nieuwe uitdagingen. Met grenzen die steeds meer vervagen –tussen landen, markten of sectoren – helpt ons wereldwijd netwerk ons om bedrijven en organisaties wegwijs te maken op onbekend terrein. Om deuren te openen bij toekomstige partners en om ingewikkelde wetten en regels te begrijpen en toe te passen. In België telt KPMG ongeveer 1.300 medewerkers. Eén voor één experts in hun vakgebied, één voor één gepassioneerde professionals. We leggen de lat hoog bij KPMG en zorgen ervoor dat onze medewerkers hun expertise en talent maximaal kunnen ontwikkelen. Of het nu gaat om juniors die nog aan de start van hun loopbaan staan of om ervaren krachten, onze mensen scholen zich het hele jaar door bij. Om op de hoogte te blijven van de laatste ontwikkelingen, om nieuwe inzichten te verwerven en door te geven, om topkwaliteit te leveren. Bij de diensten die KPMG levert, zijn het onze geëngageerde en gedreven mensen die echt het verschil maken.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5622269531.scraped: Job Description Project Overview: We are entering an era where Generative AI is no longer just a trend but a core business requirement. Our AWS AI/ML team is launching the Enterprise GenAI Accelerator project. This internship offers a unique opportunity for one student to build a production-ready "Accelerator" designed for consultancy teams to demonstrate and deploy high-tier GenAI capabilities on AWS. It provides a perfect foundation for a master's thesis, focusing on the synergy between Large Language Models (LLMs) and modern cloud automation. Project Description: The goal is to create a modular, multi-tenant-ready chatbot architecture. This project is built on two fundamental pillars: GenAI Excellence and DevOps Leadership. Using a Python-native stack (Streamlit, Boto3, LangChain), you will develop a decoupled back-end capable of sophisticated reasoning, while simultaneously building the CI/CD pipelines and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) required to deploy this solution at scale.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5619878413.scraped: Description Le Graduate Programme Finance offre une expérience diversifiée et enrichissante au travers de 3 rotations dans différents départements financiers, en France ainsi qu’à l’international au sein de nos filiales ou de nos CSP. Responsibilities Structure du programme : Rotation 1 : Auditeur Interne, basé dans notre filiale à Bruxelles (12 mois) Participer aux missions d'audit interne pour évaluer l'efficacité des processus financiers. Identifier les risques et proposer des recommandations pour améliorer les contrôles internes. Collaborer avec différentes équipes pour assurer la conformité et l'intégrité des opérations financières. Rotation 2 : Contrôle de Gestion au sein d'une filiale du groupe ou CSP (16 à 18 mois) Analyser les performances financières et préparer des rapports pour la direction. Contribuer à l'élaboration des budgets et des prévisions financières. Participer à l'optimisation des processus financiers au sein du Centre de Services Partagés (CSP). Rotation 3 : Bras droit DGF ou PMO projet Finance (6 à 8 mois) Assister le Directeur Gestion Finances (DGF) ou le Project Management Office (PMO) dans la gestion des projets stratégiques. Participer à la mise en œuvre de projets financiers clés et à la prise de décisions stratégiques. Préparer des analyses et des présentations pour soutenir les initiatives financières. Ce que nous offrons : Un environnement de travail dynamique et stimulant dans un groupe agroalimentaire familial. La possibilité d’évoluer rapidement sur des postes à responsabilités, un accélérateur de carrière pour les métiers de la Finance. A minima une expérience à l’international. L’appartenance à la communauté Graduate (mentorat, intégration, événements, formation etc.) Qualifications Profil Recherché : Diplôme Ecole de Commerce avec une spécialisation Finance Mobile géographiquement en France et à l’international Disponibilité prise de poste : Septembre 2026 Excellentes compétences analytiques et de résolution de problèmes. Capacité à travailler en équipe et à communiquer efficacement. Maîtrise des outils informatiques, notamment Excel et logiciels de gestion financière Anglais courant, une 3ème langue est un plus.

https://www.adzuna.ch/details/5619878413.scraped: Description Le Graduate Programme Finance offre une expérience diversifiée et enrichissante au travers de 3 rotations dans différents départements financiers, en France ainsi qu’à l’international au sein de nos filiales ou de nos CSP. Responsibilities Structure du programme : Rotation 1 : Auditeur Interne, basé dans notre filiale à Bruxelles (12 mois) Participer aux missions d'audit interne pour évaluer l'efficacité des processus financiers. Identifier les risques et proposer des recommandations pour améliorer les contrôles internes. Collaborer avec différentes équipes pour assurer la conformité et l'intégrité des opérations financières. Rotation 2 : Contrôle de Gestion au sein d'une filiale du groupe ou CSP (16 à 18 mois) Analyser les performances financières et préparer des rapports pour la direction. Contribuer à l'élaboration des budgets et des prévisions financières. Participer à l'optimisation des processus financiers au sein du Centre de Services Partagés (CSP). Rotation 3 : Bras droit DGF ou PMO projet Finance (6 à 8 mois) Assister le Directeur Gestion Finances (DGF) ou le Project Management Office (PMO) dans la gestion des projets stratégiques. Participer à la mise en œuvre de projets financiers clés et à la prise de décisions stratégiques. Préparer des analyses et des présentations pour soutenir les initiatives financières. Ce que nous offrons : Un environnement de travail dynamique et stimulant dans un groupe agroalimentaire familial. La possibilité d’évoluer rapidement sur des postes à responsabilités, un accélérateur de carrière pour les métiers de la Finance. A minima une expérience à l’international. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5628205040.scraped: As part of the growth of our Energy Business Unit, we are looking for a Junior Project Engineer eager to contribute to a wide range of industrial and energy-related projects. You will join Vulcain’s engineering teams and support strategic initiatives in sectors such as nuclear , electricity and gas networks , biomass , and renewable energy . This role is ideal for a young graduate who wants to develop technical and transversal project expertise within a stimulating, multidisciplinary, and future‑oriented environment. ABOUT THE POSITION: As a Project Engineer, you will be involved in the design, coordination, or follow-up of engineering activities depending on the project and client needs. Your missions may include: Supporting project teams in studies, design, and technical analyses (electricity, electromechanics, grid or power systems). Participating in the follow‑up of engineering packages and ensuring technical consistency. Contributing to the preparation and review of technical documentation. Assisting with project planning, risk management, and reporting activities. Collaborating closely with multidisciplinary teams (mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, safety, operations…). Participating in field visits, surveys, or site coordination activities. You will progressively gain autonomy and take on more responsibilities as you develop your technical and project management skills. ABOUT YOU: Master’s degree in Electromechanics or Electrical Engineering (ideally with a focus on grid , power systems , or energy‑related topics) Strong interest in the energy sector (nuclear, networks, renewables, biomass…). Fluent in Dutch and English (French can be an additional asset for specific projects) Available to start in September/October 2026 ABOUT US: Vulcain Engineering is a group specialized in the energy sector that supports companies active in Renewable & Nuclear Energy, Gas & Infrastructure through our Study Offices and Consultancy services. Vulcain Engineering Group is positioned as a transformation partner thanks to its ultra-specialization in strategic sectors which positively impact the transformation of our society and supports its clients in their transformation towards more sustainable and responsible models. Vulcain supports its clients throughout the entire life cycle of their projects, from design to commissioning, by integrating constructability and safety requirements right from the design phase. In Belgium, our activities focus on consultancy services, delivering expertise in three main areas: Energy/Infrastructure Gas, Renewables & Processes Life Sciences With headquarters based in Brussels, and clients spread all over the country, we address key projects in the Belgian industry, offering our consultants the opportunity to work on challenging topics and discover different areas during their time at Vulcain. Why would you apply? An annual gross salary ranging from 37 000€ to 39 000€ paid over 13,92 months and advantages such as a company car (and fuel card), representation fees, insurances, meal vouchers, eco cheques, a sectorial bonus (the salary indicated corresponds to the level of experience mentioned in the job description. For any other level of experience, the annual salary may vary accordingly) A tailored career path within the company : opportunities to discover different business units, training, follow-up from your business manager, and a development evaluation every year A human-based company with slightly more than 120 consultants and synergies between the different business units

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5618208740.scraped: Informations générales Référence 2026-1007 Métier SUPPORT - IT/Infrastructure Intitulé de la fonction DEVOPS ENGINEER (LIEGE-BELGIUM) Contrat CDI Description de la fonction THE SCOPE With 30 years of history, today Gaming1 is one of the international leaders in both land-based and online games of chance (casino games, sports betting and poker). Its evolution at the heart of innovation is accompanied by a diversification and specialization of positions, with the creation of new cutting-edge jobs. As part of its sustained growth, Gaming1 is looking for a DevOps Engineer to join its IT team This position is part of the Gaming1 IT department, which designs, delivers, and operates technical solutions for all land-based establishments of the group (casinos, gaming halls, sports betting centers) as well as corporate functions such as Finance, Human Resources, Legal, and Compliance. YOUR ROLE You won’t just “maintain servers.” You will treat our internal platform as a premium product, empowering development teams across the organization to ship code faster, safer, and with confidence. Own the Platform : Design, evolve, and drive the roadmap of a secure, scalable, and resilient platform ecosystem supporting both retail and corporate use cases. Empower Builders: Create self-service capabilities and automation that simplify and accelerate developers’ daily work. Innovate & Evolve: Research new tools, implement Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC), and promote platform engineering best practices across IT. Protect & Serve : Ensure systems meet the highest standards of security, availability, and ethical responsibility, in line with regulatory, compliance, and player safety requirements. Profil YOUR PROFIL We hire for mindset and potential first. You are a strong fit if: You are an Automator: You avoid manual repetition—if it can be scripted, you automate it. You are Linux & Container Native: You are comfortable in the shell and understand how Docker and modern containerization work under the hood. You are a Bridge Builder: You speak both “Ops” (stability, security, reliability) and “Dev” (velocity, features) and know how to connect them. You Care About Quality: You value clean implementations, solid documentation, and resilient systems that let teams sleep at night. English Proficiency: You can communicate technical concepts clearly in English (B2+). The Tech Stack (What We Use) You don’t need to master everything from day one, but curiosity and willingness to grow are essential. Core: Linux, Docker, Python or .NET Data & Messaging: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, NServiceBus Ops Tools: Ansible, GitLab CI, OpenTelemetry, Grafana Security: Keycloak, Vault WHAT WE OFFER As well as having a unique experience - centered around our values of pleasure, team spirit, performance, boldness and integrity - alongside 1,600 employees around the world, we offer: the opportunity to contribute to the international development of a family company from Liège the chance to take part in a human and entrepreneurial adventure within a solid group a friendly work environment that is adapted to reaching optimal performance leadership by talents, values, trust and autonomy the opportunity to progress and specialize with our internal mobility system a competitive salary package with several perks The possibility to work remotely up to 50% for a good work-life balance Ready to level up? Send your CV and tell us briefly: What is the one technical challenge you are most proud of solving? THE INTERVIEW PROCESS Lieu Liège Critères candidat Niveau hiérarchique LK Confirmé

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5614812720.scraped: Job Description Are you passionate about finance, digitalisation , and looking to gain hands-on experience in a global IT Controlling environment ? Join our dynamic and international Group IT Finance Controlling team , where you will contribute to financial planning, digital transformation, and tool optimisation across a growing network of companies. As an IT Finance Controlling & Digitalisation Intern , you will work at the intersection of finance and technology, supporting both the Group IT Finance Controlling team in Brussels and collaborating with the IT Development team . You will contribute to the budgeting, forecasting, reporting , and digitalisation of financial processes , to increase accuracy, efficiency, and transparency across the network. Key Responsibilities Assist in the annual budget cycle, forecasts, and mid-term planning for IT expenses. Support monthly reporting by generating financial statements, updating management reports, and tracking KPIs. Help with variance analysis on budget deviations. Collaborate on financial digitalisation initiatives by testing new tools and improving existing systems. Conduct pilot testing and troubleshooting for financial tools and dashboards. Guide end-users through new tools and updates , and collect feedback for future improvements. Assist in purchase order management and approval processes. Implement structural changes of master data (including the creation and review of Business Units, and Business Unit Clusters) and oversee the status throughout. Duration: 6 months. Starting date: July/August/September (flexible). This internship is compensated. An internship agreement with your university is required.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5611021212.scraped: Young Graduates In Technology Resources Are you dynamic, driven and ambitious & graduating in summer? Does the sound of working in a hub of innovation appeal to you? Are you keen to be at the forefront of new tech developments? Do you see yourself working in a large international company? If you answered “yes” (who can blame you?) four times, then you should keep on reading. Your Job As a Functional Analyst at Accenture, you will play a crucial role as the trusted advisor for our clients, helping them navigate through any difficulties or problems they might encounter. Your exceptional analytical skills and problem-solving abilities will be key in finding effective solutions for these challenges and ensuring their successful implementation. You will be responsible for creating design documentation that outlines the proposed solutions, ensuring clear communication and alignment between stakeholders and the implementation team. Additionally, you will take an active role in testing your designs after its implementation to ensure its correctness. You will develop a deep understanding of the industry's business processes, enabling you to provide valuable insights and recommendations to our clients, contributing to their growth and success. Your day-to-day responsibilities will include active participation in daily meetings with the functional and technical analysts you are collaborating with, as well as weekly meetings with stakeholders and key users. During these meetings, you will discuss the status of ongoing projects, address dilemmas, and collaboratively develop solutions. At Accenture, we value diversity and recognize that your role as a Functional Analyst will require a broad range of skills. With your positive attitude and strong team orientation, you will utilize your creative and analytical abilities to assist our clients by applying new methodologies, processes, and technologies. Your keen eye for problem-solving and your knack for identifying innovative solutions will be instrumental in making our projects succeed. Your team Accenture leverages design thinking, industry insights and the latest digital and Security methodologies to help clients innovate, grow and improve their businesses. Our expertise, capabilities and experience mean that our clients (including some of the biggest brands in the world) trust us to find the right solutions for their needs. Together with our talented international colleagues, we focus on connecting the needs of businesses with the new possibilities that come with technological progress. Because that's where the real challenges are: inventing and testing things that have never been tried before, getting new applications ready for roll-out, and ultimately guiding clients to select and implement the right technologies to transform their businesses. Your Background You’re studying a bachelor or master’s degree in any educational background and will be graduating in summer 2025; You’re perfectly fluent in English, Dutch/French and can express yourself in French/Dutch; You are a typical go-getter with an infectious can-do attitude; You have a passion for business and a keen interest in technology; You have strong analytical skills with the ability to identify and solve complex problems; You have excellent organizational skills and can manage multiple tasks simultaneously; You are flexible to adapt to changing priorities and deliver high-quality results under tight deadlines; You are curious and pro-active You are a true team player who works to the best of your abilities and inspires colleagues along the way; You thrive in a diverse working environment comprising employees from all walks of life, cultures and creeds. Our offer Accenture is an incredible place to work - and keep learning. By joining us, you’ll become part of a global company with a world-class brand and reputation. Besides the work we do for our clients, we’re really proud of our vibrant, diverse workplace culture: we believe in openness and honesty, fairness and equality, common sense and realism. We want to get to know the real you and help you explore and grow - whatever it is you're great at. So you will always have lots of learning opportunities (formal and informal) to improve your role-specific skills and expertise. Besides our high-profile, challenging projects and our nurturing work environment, we offer excellent employee benefits, including: 13th month + holiday allowance 20 + 12 holidays Laptop and smartphone Hospitalization insurance and extensive group insurance package Bonus program Training & learning opportunities Green mobility program: e-bikes, public transport, bike 2 work allowance,… Flexrewards: decide on your rewards package with our flexible benefits tool Discount program: get discounts at your favorite (online) shops Employee shares purchase plan Eco-cheques And many more! Interested? Are you ready to join Accenture for a career where you can be yourself and do what you love? Apply now and change the world around you. About Accenture Accenture is a leading global professional services company that helps the world’s leading businesses, governments and other organizations build their digital core, optimize their operations, accelerate revenue growth and enhance citizen services—creating tangible value at speed and scale. We are a talent- and innovation-led company with approximately 791,000 people serving clients in more than 120 countries. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5628203320.scraped: As part of the growth of our life sciences activities, we are looking for a junior engineer who will support our clients in the pharmaceutical , biotech , and medical device industries. You will join project teams involved in activities related to quality , process engineering , and production operations , contributing to the reliability, compliance, and performance of critical manufacturing environments. This position is ideal for a young graduate who wants to develop strong expertise in regulated industries and grow in a field where quality, processes, and innovation are at the core of every project. ABOUT THE POSITION: Depending on the project needs, your missions may include: Supporting quality‑related activities: documentation, deviation management, CAPA, change control, batch review… Assisting process engineering tasks: process mapping, equipment follow‑up, process improvements, data review, sampling plans… Participating in production support: troubleshooting, monitoring, continuous improvement, shop‑floor coordination. Contributing to GMP‑compliant documentation: SOPs, protocols, reports, traceability records. Supporting validation or qualification activities (process, equipment, utilities, cleaning, CSV…). Collaborating with multidisciplinary teams (QA, QC, production, engineering, validation, HSE…). Participating in audits, inspections, or preparation for regulatory readiness when applicable. As your experience grows, you will progressively gain autonomy and expand your technical and regulatory scope. ABOUT YOU: Master’s degree in Industrial, Electromechanics, Bioscience Engineering, Biochemistry, Pharmacy, or Drug Development A first exposure to GMP environments (internship, thesis, student job…) is highly appreciated . Structured, reliable, and detail‑oriented working style. Analytical mindset with the ability to understand and document complex technical processes. Fluent in Dutch and English Available to start in September/October 2026 ABOUT US: Vulcain Engineering is a group specialized in the energy sector that supports companies active in Renewable & Nuclear Energy, Gas & Infrastructure through our Study Offices and Consultancy services. Vulcain Engineering Group is positioned as a transformation partner thanks to its ultra-specialization in strategic sectors which positively impact the transformation of our society and supports its clients in their transformation towards more sustainable and responsible models. Vulcain supports its clients throughout the entire life cycle of their projects, from design to commissioning, by integrating constructability and safety requirements right from the design phase. In Belgium, our activities focus on consultancy services, delivering expertise in three main areas: Energy/Infrastructure Gas, Renewables & Processes Life Sciences With headquarters based in Brussels, and clients spread all over the country, we address key projects in the Belgian industry, offering our consultants the opportunity to work on challenging topics and discover different areas during their time at Vulcain. Why would you apply? An annual gross salary ranging from 37 000€ to 39 000€ paid over 13,92 months and advantages such as a company car (and fuel card), representation fees, insurances, meal vouchers, eco cheques, a sectorial bonus (the salary indicated corresponds to the level of experience mentioned in the job description. For any other level of experience, the annual salary may vary accordingly) A tailored career path within the company : opportunities to discover different business units, training, follow-up from your business manager, and a development evaluation every year A human-based company with slightly more than 120 consultants and synergies between the different business units

You will start on a permanent contract and, during your first year, be enrolled in our dedicated Young Graduate Program, designed to support your professional development as you begin your career. Depending on the assignment, you could be working in areas such as: (Electro)Mechanics and Mechatronics Energy & Renewables Automotive and related engineering fields Automation and industrial or advanced systems The Young Graduate Program runs alongside your main assignment, providing structured support, guidance, workshops, training, and networking opportunities to help you develop your technical skills, professional experience, and connections. Program highlights Collaborate on a side project with other young graduates Attend workshops and training to strengthen professional and soft skills Participate in internal events to build your network Be part of a people-oriented environment that values long-term development After your first year, you continue your career at TMC, with ongoing support and opportunities tailored to your growth. What we expect from you You are graduating this year with a Master’s degree in Engineering or Computer Science You are motivated to learn and develop in a professional environment You have an interest in industrial, technical environments, sustainable energy or future-focused technologies You speak English and French or Dutch , with at least a basic understanding of a third language This opening focuses on opportunities in Brussels, Wallonia, and Limburg. What you can expect from us TMC is an international high-tech consultancy company that brings expertise to their clients in order to support them with the realization of their technological challenges. As such, TMC is operating as an expert center at the disposition of its clients and we help them to be more competitive in an ever more global and faster changing technological world. At TMC we believe that people are the driving force behind technological innovation. That is why we want to create the best possible conditions for tech talents to thrive in. We offer you a challenging and stimulating work environment in which you can be the director of your own career. As an Employeneur you are part of our TMC family. Next to our outstanding technical expertise, fun and engagement are meaningful parts of our culture.

We need you for the position of Intern – Test Automation Engineer The Job We are offering a full-time internship in test automation within a dynamic and innovative team. In this role, you will design, develop, and maintain automated tests to ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of our software products. You will collaborate closely with developers, product managers, and test engineers, contributing directly to real projects while gaining hands-on experience with modern tools and methodologies. This internship is designed for motivated individuals who want to learn, grow, and make a tangible impact in a professional environment. Your mission As a test automation intern, your mission will be to learn while actively contributing to the team’s success. You will: Develop and maintain automated test scripts using Python Execute tests, analyze results, and identify defects Collaborate with development teams to help resolve issues Ensure adequate test coverage and maintain clear test documentation As an intern, you will be fully integrated into the team and involved in real projects from day one. Your work will have a direct impact on product quality, and you will be encouraged to take ownership of tasks and solve concrete technical challenges. This role will allow you to strengthen your skills in Python, Git, and Linux while gaining practical experience in a professional software development environment. Curiosity, initiative, and motivation will be key to your success and growth. Your qualifications We are looking for a motivated candidate with the following background: Knowledge of Python 3 Experience with Git Basic understanding of Linux terminal commands Strong curiosity and willingness to learn new technologies Ability to work independently with a proactive mindset Prior professional experience is not required; motivation, autonomy, and eagerness to grow are essential. Our Offer We offer a full-time internship of 3 to 6 months, with flexibility to adapt to your academic schedule. Throughout the internship, you will benefit from regular guidance and close collaboration with experienced team members, allowing you to develop both technical and professional skills. You will gain hands-on experience in test automation using industry-standard tools while working in a supportive and collaborative environment. Our offices are based in a vibrant, welcoming environment, and we look forward to seeing you soon!

This role will allow you to strengthen your skills in Python, Git, and Linux while gaining practical experience in a professional software development environment. Curiosity, initiative, and motivation will be key to your success and growth. Your qualifications We are looking for a motivated candidate with the following background: Knowledge of Python 3 Experience with Git Basic understanding of Linux terminal commands Strong curiosity and willingness to learn new technologies Ability to work independently with a proactive mindset Prior professional experience is not required; motivation, autonomy, and eagerness to grow are essential. Our Offer We offer a full-time internship of 3 to 6 months, with flexibility to adapt to your academic schedule. Throughout the internship, you will benefit from regular guidance and close collaboration with experienced team members, allowing you to develop both technical and professional skills. You will gain hands-on experience in test automation using industry-standard tools while working in a supportive and collaborative environment. Our offices are based in a vibrant, welcoming environment, and we look forward to seeing you soon!

Tu veux un job où ton expertise technique a un impact direct sur la performance et la sécurité de l’infrastructure ? Alors cette opportunité est pour toi Linux System Administrator - Tes responsabilités Tu seras un acteur clé de la stabilité, de la performance et de la sécurité de notre infrastructure IT. Administrer, maintenir et optimiser des environnements Linux (production, test, staging). Assurer le monitoring des systèmes et intervenir proactivement en cas d’incident. Diagnostiquer et résoudre les incidents de niveau 2/3 avec efficacité. Gérer les serveurs (physiques & virtuels), le stockage et les sauvegardes. Automatiser les tâches récurrentes via scripting (Bash, Python ou autres). Participer aux projets d’évolution d’infrastructure (migration, cloud, conteneurisation). Collaborer avec les équipes DevOps et développement pour améliorer la performance et la fiabilité. Garantir la sécurité des systèmes (patching, hardening, gestion des accès). Linux System Administrator - Ton profil Diplôme en informatique ou expérience équivalente (minimum 3 années) Excellente maîtrise des environnements Linux (RedHat, CentOS, Ubuntu ou autres). Bonne connaissance des services réseau (DNS, DHCP, HTTP, SSH, etc.). Expérience avec la virtualisation (VMware, KVM) et/ou le cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP) est un atout. Connaissances en scripting (Bash, Python). Familiarité avec Docker, Kubernetes ou outils DevOps ? Un vrai plus. Tu parles français et anglais (le néerlandais est un plus). Esprit analytique, autonomie et excellent sens de la collaboration. Linux System Administrator - L'offre et les avantages Une scale-up technologique innovante. Un environnement technique stimulant avec de vrais défis. Des opportunités concrètes d’évolution (expert, DevOps, architecture). Un package salarial attractif + avantages extra-légaux. Une équipe passionnée, collaborative et ambitieuse.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5628204888.scraped: Are you passionate about cloud technologies and infrastructure automation? We’re looking for a motivated Cloud Engineer to join a dynamic IT team in a global, fast-paced environment. This is your chance to work with cutting-edge cloud solutions while contributing to scalable, secure, and cost-effective infrastructure. What You’ll Do Design, deploy, and manage cloud infrastructure, primarily in Microsoft Azure (AWS knowledge is a plus). Develop and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform and Git workflows . Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps; GitHub knowledge is a bonus). Collaborate with internal IT teams to meet infrastructure needs across multiple business areas. Support containerized workloads using Kubernetes and related services. Contribute to FinOps practices for cloud cost optimization and transparency. Ensure proper documentation and adherence to security, compliance, and governance standards. Continuously learn and implement new technologies and industry best practices. What We’re Looking For 1–7 years of experience in Cloud Engineering, DevOps, or related roles . Hands-on experience with Microsoft Azure ; AWS knowledge is a plus. Strong skills in Terraform , Git , and Infrastructure as Code practices. Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines ( Azure DevOps preferred). Understanding of IaaS, PaaS, SaaS models , cloud security, and compliance principles. Experience or interest in Kubernetes . Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills . Adaptable, proactive, and curious, with a willingness to learn in a fast-paced environment. Nice to Have Experience in a regulated industry (e.g., pharmaceutical, healthcare, finance). Certifications in Azure, AWS, Terraform, or related cloud technologies. Experience deploying production applications to cloud environments.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5608562565.scraped: Stagiaire Analyste Fonctionnel À propos de Wavenet À la recherche d’un stage formateur et stimulant dans un cadre humain et bienveillant ? Chez Wavenet , nous développons des solutions IT sur mesure pour des secteurs aussi variés que l’industrie, les banques, les services publics… Du portail e-Gov à l’intelligence artificielle, en passant par la data et les solutions web : chaque projet est unique, tout comme les personnes qui y contribuent. Aujourd’hui, nous cherchons un(e) stagiaire Analyste Fonctionnel/Business Analyste pour rejoindre notre équipe dynamique. Ton quotidien chez Wavenet ? Sous la supervision de nos analystes expérimentés, tu auras l’occasion de : • Participer à la collecte et l’analyse des besoins métiers auprès de nos clients • Rédiger et structurer des spécifications fonctionnelles (User stories, UML, ...) • Contribuer à la modélisation de processus et à la documentation des flux métiers (BPMN) • Découvrir les méthodes de travail en Agile et les outils collaboratifs (Azur DevOps, Microsoft365, etc.) • Collaborer avec des profils variés (chefs de projet, développeurs, testeurs, UX/UI, …) • T’initier aux bonnes pratiques d’ analyse Profil recherché • Tu es étudiant(e) en master informatique, gestion, business analyste ou ingénierie • Tu es curieux(se) et intéressé(e) par la compréhension des besoins métiers et leur traduction en solutions IT • Tu aimes analyser, structurer et communiquer clairement tes idées • Tu aimes être en contact avec le client, les développeurs et les chefs de projet • Tu es motivé(e), organisé(e) et apprécies le travail en équipe • Une première approche de la modélisation (UML, BPMN, wireframes) est un plus Pourquoi faire ton stage chez Wavenet ? • Pour travailler sur des projets concrets et variés , qui te donneront une vraie expérience terrain • Pour être guidé(e) par un mentor, entouré(e) d’une équipe passionnée et disponible • Pour évoluer dans une entreprise à taille humaine , où chacun a sa place • Pour une ambiance conviviale et professionnelle Infos pratiques Lieu du stage : Leuze-en-Hainaut (télétravail possible) Durée : à définir selon ta convention de stage (minimum 3 mois idéalement) Convention de stage obligatoire Intéressé(e) ? Envoie ton CV et ta demande de stage à jobs@wavenet.be On a hâte de te rencontrer et de t’accompagner dans cette première expérience !

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5628204939.scraped: We are currently looking for a "Test Automation Engineer" to strengthen the Cronos Europa team Location : Near-site (accessible within 2 hours from Brussels) - Remote work possible Responsabilities: Definition of test plans and test cases. Construction of test scenarios and scripts. Execution of tests. Preparation and maintenance of test data. Test reporting. Automated testing Enable the transition to an automated testing approach. Evaluate tools and technologies for automation best fit to the project within the DevSecOps approach. Create an approach and methodology for building a test automation architecture. Design, prepare and monitor automated tests. Design and develop new or modified test automation solutions that meet business needs. Create automated test reporting and metrics collection. Support business stakeholders during acceptance testing. Technical skills: Experience in manual testing. Experience in test automation (minimum 3 years). Experience in test data preparation. Experience (at least 3 years) working in Agile/Sprint-based IT project methodologies. Experience (at least 1 year) with data quality checking using SQL or similar languages in databases and/or data lakes. Good knowledge of data-driven projects and data-driven solutions testing (data warehouse, data lakes, data mining, etc.). Good knowledge of testing technologies, tools, frameworks and techniques, particularly in cloud environments. Knowledge of: Test Management Tools (e.g. Zephyr for JIRA). Defect Tracking tools, in particular JIRA / Zephyr for JIRA. Web application testing tools such as WebdriverIO, Selenium or Protractor. API testing tools such as SOAPUI. Relational database management systems (MS SQL, Oracle). Public cloud technologies. Knowledge of Elasticsearch, Spark, Machine Learning, Deep Learning and Artificial Intelligence technologies is an asset. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills. Good redaction skills with experience in preparing written reports. Strong analysis and problem-solving skills. Ability to write clear and structured documents. Ability to cope with fast-changing technologies used in application development. Profile : Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in IT, Business Management, or a related field. Ability to analyze complex issues and develop effective solutions. Good communication and interpersonal skills. Fluency in English is required, French is a strong asset (B1/B2). Capability of integration in an international and multicultural environment, with rapid self-starting capability and strong team spirit. Ability to participate in multilingual meetings and work on multiple large projects simultaneously. Excellent team player. High degree of discretion and integrity is required due to the management of personal and confidential data. Certifications: ISTQB Specialist Level Test Automation Engineer ISTQB Specialist Level Test Acceptance Testing ISTQB Advanced Level Test Analyst Any equivalent certification subject to approval by the requesting unit Why Cronos Group? We’ll propose you: An attractive salary package A good work-life balance environment The assurance of working in cutting-edge technologies in an entrepreneurial spirit. The opportunity to develop your skills thanks to tailor-made training courses according to your needs A good job in a friendly place If you wish to integrate a dynamic structure on a human scale while working with the latest technologies, don't wait anymore and join Cronos!

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5608562867.scraped: Welcome at Yuzzu! Yuzzu, an insurance broker, offers a dynamic and welcoming work environment, where clear communication and respect for our commitments are at the heart of our culture. As a member of the AXA Group, we place the quality of human relationships and innovation at the center of our priorities. This is reflected in our values of dynamism, simplicity, and empathy. Would you like to invest your energy and talent in a growing company? Take a look at our vacancies and apply by sending your CV and cover letter to recruitment@yuzzu.be! Yuzzu is currently seeking a DevOps Engineer Which are your most important targets? Yuzzu is constantly launching new projects and initiatives aiming to innovate, meet the expectations of clients and internal users, and prepare for the future by modernizing its IT/application landscape. With this in mind, several multidisciplinary teams are continuously working together with a cross-functional technical team on these projects, based on an "Agile/Scrum" approach. Which are your responsibilities? As DevOps Engineer you will oversee the implementation and administration of various application hosting platforms with the support of AXA Group expert teams. You’ll manage the automation of integration processes, of testing, of application deployment and all other settings of the available platforms. You’ll collaborate closely with our other Agile teams, Business and Support teams on fixing incidents, problems which could occur on the application platforms. You’ll guarantee the robustness of the platform based on defined recommendations. As team member of the Transversal Technical Team, actively supporting our internal and external customers, you’ll have an active role when sharing your ideas and efforts to continuously improve the deliveries of the team and the deliveries of the company. You’re enthusiastic about participating to our Cloudification journey. What are your skills and experiences? You have a degree in computer science (bachelor's, master's, or equivalent) Setup and administration of Linux system (Debian, Ubuntu, RHEL) Scripting: Bash, PowerShell, Python, … Containerization and Infrastructure As Code: Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, Terraform Knowledge on Cloud Infrastructure and SaaS Services (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, …) Monitoring and logging: basic knowledge of Kibana, Grafana, Prometheus, etc You are comfortable with containerization environments (Docker, OpenShift, Kubernetes, Helm, Skaffold, …) and infrastructure as code principles You have in-depth knowledge in at least one of the cloud environments (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and cloud service integration You are proficient in scripting languages such as Bash, PowerShell, Python, or others. You are familiar with setting up automation, deployments via CI/CD (Jenkins), as well as automation tools (such as Terraform, Puppet, ...) You are familiar with the needs of monitoring, logging, and traceability of platforms (using tools such as Kibana, Grafana, Prometheus, ...) You have some understanding of managing complex platforms Assets Experience in creating and managing Cloud Resources Experience in creating and maintaining Pipeline (GitHub Actions, Jenkins) Experience in managing databases such as Oracle, MSSQL and PostgreSQL Experience in managing Java applications Participation on Open-Source projects What’s your profile? Flexibility is in your genes: managing changes and priorities is part of your daily routine You are interested in new technologies You have already worked in an Agile team You are focused on business needs and align your priorities with them As a DevOps Engineer, you have an analytical mind. Creativity and innovation are part of your qualities Thanks to your team spirit and communication skills, you maintain excellent relationships both internally and externally Dynamic and resourceful, open and curious, you work independently and proactively, and have a sense of responsibility You are inspired, passionate, and inspiring To work at Yuzzu, any experience in accident, fire, and risk insurance is an additional asset You are proficient in English and at least one of the two national languages (Dutch or French) What do we offer? A competitive salary with extra-legal benefits A friendly and open working atmosphere in a pleasant environment A diverse and motivating role at the heart of our company Autonomy based on your skills A good work-life balance, thanks to hybrid working setup Ongoing training and support Who is Yuzzu? Yuzzu is the Belgian market leader in direct insurance. Member of the Axa Group, Yuzzu is market leader in direct insurance in Belgium. Every day, our 170 staff are on hand to guarantee a quality and personalised service for our 150,000 customers and our partners. Yuzzu today remains a company that operates on a human scale. At its offices in the Tour & Taxis building in Brussels and at the 10 shops located throughout Belgium, the atmosphere is friendly and informal, reflecting the spirit of a young and growing company. Yuzzu attaches great importance to its staff and the quality of interpersonal relations. Because it takes a solid team to meet everyday challenges and translate them into effective and innovative solutions for our customers. Do you want to be part of our team? Apply now!

At its offices in the Tour & Taxis building in Brussels and at the 10 shops located throughout Belgium, the atmosphere is friendly and informal, reflecting the spirit of a young and growing company. Yuzzu attaches great importance to its staff and the quality of interpersonal relations. Because it takes a solid team to meet everyday challenges and translate them into effective and innovative solutions for our customers. Do you want to be part of our team? Apply now!
