innovants dans l'e-gouvernement et l'e-santé pour les institutions de la sécurité sociale et des soins de santé. Pour Smals, "ICT for society" n'est pas qu'un slogan : tous nos projets profitent à la société. Smals contribue au portail de la sécurité sociale et à la plateforme eHealth. Les projets novateurs sont notre spécialité : Student@Work, Orgadon, digitalisation des prescriptions médicales, consultation de l'historique de la carrière, transmission plus rapide des résultats de test à son médecin... En tant que Test Automation Engineer, vous soutenez les équipes de développement par la mise en place et la maintenance de cadres d'automatisation de tests et de scripts de test. Vous créez et gérez des scripts de test pour des tests fonctionnels, de régression et de performance. Vous élaborez un plan de test et garantissez la qualité de votre mission de testing. Vous veillez à ce que les tests automatisés soient parfaitement intégrés dans les pipelines de Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD), afin que les nouveaux updates software puissent être testés de manière continue et fiable. L'analyse des résultats des tests constitue une part importante de votre travail. Vous évaluez soigneusement les résultats des tests et rapportez vos conclusions à l'équipe de développement. Les éventuels problèmes peuvent ainsi être rapidement identifiés et résolus, ce qui contribue à la stabilité et à la qualité du software. Parallèlement, vous collaborez intensivement avec les développeurs, les spécialistes QA et les autres parties prenantes. En travaillant ensemble pour améliorer les stratégies de test et appliquer les meilleures pratiques, vous garantissez que les produits software répondent à des normes de qualité élevées et fonctionnent de manière optimale. Vous êtes titulaire d'un diplôme de bachelor en informatique ou possédez une expertise équivalente. Vous avez également une première expérience dans le domaine des tests. Vous réalisez des tests automatisés sur des services SOAP et REST et avez de l'expérience avec Java et SQL. Vous travaillez avec des outils tels que Selenium, Cucumber et Postman et de préférence aussi avec JUnit, Lombok, XML et des IDE. Une expérience de la gestion des versions constitue un atout. De plus, vous êtes habitué à travailler dans un environnement et selon la méthodologie Agile. Vous avez également une bonne connaissance des plateformes cloud. Votre certification ISTQB Foundation et ISTQB Certified Test Automation Engineer est un atout certain. Vous possédez de solides compétences en matière de résolution de problèmes et êtes capable d'analyser et de résoudre rapidement et efficacement des défis techniques. Grâce à votre esprit d'analyse, vous êtes capable d'évaluer en profondeur le comportement d'un software et les résultats de tests ainsi que d'en tirer des enseignements précieux. Vous avez également de bonnes capacités de communication. Ainsi, vous êtes capable de communiquer efficacement avec les différentes parties prenantes d'un projet. Vous possédez une bonne connaissance du néerlandais et l'anglais. - Nous vous offrons un emploi varié, stimulant et socialement engagé vous permettant de contribuer à la réalisation de solutions informatiques ayant un impact sur la société. « ICT for society » n'est pas notre slogan par hasard. - Vous recherchez un bon équilibre vie privée - vie professionnelle ? Profitez de nos horaires de travail flexibles et de notre politique de télétravail très étendue. - Nous offrons de nombreuses possibilités de formation interne et externe vous permettant de perfectionner en permanence vos compétences techniques et vos

active:bg-adzuna-green-700 text-center block m-4 md:mx-0 md:my-4 md:inline-block " Solliciteren /a section class="adp-body mx-4 mb-4 text-sm md:mx-0 md:text-base md:mb-0" Smals réalise des projets ICT innovants dans l'e-gouvernement et l'e-santé pour les institutions de la sécurité sociale et des soins de santé. Pour Smals, "ICT for society" n'est pas qu'un slogan : tous nos projets profitent à la société. Smals contribue au portail de la sécurité sociale et à la plateforme eHealth. Les projets novateurs sont notre spécialité : Student@Work, Orgadon, digitalisation des prescriptions médicales, consultation de l'historique de la carrière, transmission plus rapide des résultats de test à son médecin... En tant que Test Automation Engineer, vous soutenez les équipes de développement par la mise en place et la maintenance de cadres d'automatisation de tests et de scripts de test. Vous créez et gérez des scripts de test pour des tests fonctionnels, de régression et de performance. Vous élaborez un plan de test et garantissez la qualité de votre mission de testing. Vous veillez à ce que les tests automatisés soient parfaitement intégrés dans les pipelines de Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD), afin que les nouveaux updates software puissent être testés de manière continue et fiable. L'analyse des résultats des tests constitue une part importante de votre travail. Vous évaluez soigneusement les résultats des tests et rapportez vos conclusions à l'équipe de développement. Les éventuels problèmes peuvent ainsi être rapidement identifiés et résolus, ce qui contribue à la stabilité et à la qualité du software. Parallèlement, vous collaborez intensivement avec les développeurs, les spécialistes QA et les autres parties prenantes. En travaillant ensemble pour améliorer les stratégies de test et appliquer les meilleures pratiques, vous garantissez que les produits software répondent à des normes de qualité élevées et fonctionnent de manière optimale. Vous êtes titulaire d'un diplôme de bachelor en informatique ou possédez une expertise équivalente. Vous avez également une première expérience dans le domaine des tests. Vous réalisez des tests automatisés sur des services SOAP et REST et avez de l'expérience avec Java et SQL. Vous travaillez avec des outils tels que Selenium, Cucumber et Postman et de préférence aussi avec JUnit, Lombok, XML et des IDE. Une expérience de la gestion des versions constitue un atout. De plus, vous êtes habitué à travailler dans un environnement et selon la méthodologie Agile. Vous avez également une bonne connaissance des plateformes cloud. Votre certification ISTQB Foundation et ISTQB Certified Test Automation Engineer est un atout certain. Vous possédez de solides compétences en matière de résolution de problèmes et êtes capable d'analyser et de résoudre rapidement et efficacement des défis techniques. Grâce à votre esprit d'analyse, vous êtes capable d'évaluer en profondeur le comportement d'un software et les résultats de tests ainsi que d'en tirer des enseignements précieux. Vous avez également de bonnes capacités de communication. Ainsi, vous êtes capable de communiquer efficacement avec les différentes parties prenantes d'un projet. Vous possédez une bonne connaissance du néerlandais et l'anglais. - Nous vous offrons un emploi varié, stimulant et socialement engagé vous permettant de contribuer à la réalisation de solutions informatiques ayant un impact sur la société. « ICT for society » n'est pas notre slogan par hasard. - Vous recherchez un bon équilibre vie privée - vie professionnelle ? Profitez de nos horaires de travail flexibles et de notre politique de télétravail très étendue. - Nous offrons de nombreuses possibilités de formation interne et externe vous permettant de perfectionner en permanence vos compétences techniques et vos

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5621467325.scraped: We're looking for an experienced Test Automation Engineer who is passionate about quality, automation, and working closely with development teams to deliver reliable, high-quality software. what you'll do Own end-to-end quality: from the first line of code to production release Design, build, and maintain automation frameworks using Selenium (Java) Develop and execute API automation tests (REST, integrations, non-UI testing) Implement BDD frameworks using Cucumber/Gherkin Integrate automated tests into CI/CD pipelines Analyze test results, report defects with clear logs, and support faster decision-making Work closely with developers to identify automation opportunities early Ensure test coverage across multiple environments (staging, pre-prod, prod) Maintain high standards through ISTQB best practices and documentation what we're looking for 6+ years of experience in Software Testing / Test Automation Strong hands-on experience with Selenium + Java Solid experience in API testing (REST APIs, automation frameworks) Experience with BDD tools (Cucumber, Gherkin) Good understanding of CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GIT, etc.) Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments Ability to debug, troubleshoot, and improve automation stability Strong collaboration and communication skills nice to have ISTQB certification Experience with non-functional testing Exposure to cloud environments and test data management language & education English required (French or Dutch is a plus) Bachelor's/Master's degree or equivalent hands-on experience

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5620270426.scraped: À propos de l'entreprise La Clinique Saint-Jean s’engage dans une transformation numérique majeure avec le déploiement d’un dossier patient intégré (EPIC) en collaboration avec les Cliniques Universitaires Saint-Luc. Ce projet implique une refonte et une harmonisation profondes des applications, des processus et des flux de données entre les deux hôpitaux. Il offre aux professionnels des TIC une opportunité unique de participer à la création d’un environnement numérique intégré, ambitieux et orienté vers l’avenir. À propos du rôle Votre mission: L'ingénieur système informatique assure le support et la maintenance de l'infrastructure serveur, des environnements de travail des utilisateurs finaux et effectue des tâches dans le domaine des réseaux. Ce poste requiert une personne proactive dotée d'une solide expérience technique, d'excellentes compétences en matière de résolution de problèmes et d'une grande soif d'apprendre. Responsabilités Gestion et maintenance des environnements Windows Server (Active Directory, stratégie de groupe, DNS, DHCP). Gestion des plateformes de virtualisation VMware (vSphere, ESXi). Mise en œuvre et surveillance des solutions de sauvegarde et de reprise après sinistre à l'aide de Veeam Backup & Replication. Configuration et maintenance de Microsoft System Center Configuration Management (SCCM, Intune). Veiller au respect des meilleures pratiques en matière de sécurité dans tous les systèmes. Fournir une assistance pour Microsoft Intune pour la gestion des appareils et la sécurité des terminaux. Résolution de problèmes liés à des infrastructures complexes et assistance de 2e/3e niveau. Participation à des projets d'infrastructure et contribution aux décisions architecturales. Qualifications Master en technologies de l'information, en informatique ou équivalent grâce à une expérience pertinente. Minimum 3 à 5 ans d'expérience en ingénierie des systèmes informatiques. Connaissance approfondie de : l'administration de Windows Server, la virtualisation VMware, les solutions de sauvegarde Veeam, la suite Microsoft System Center. Connaissance de : la sécurité et la conformité des terminaux, Microsoft Intune et la gestion moderne des appareils. Compréhension des principes fondamentaux des réseaux et des technologies de stockage. Expérience en script PowerShell pour l'automatisation, connaissance des plateformes cloud (Azure, AWS) ou certifications telles que ITIL, MCSA, VMware VCP sont un atout. Capacité à travailler de manière autonome et en équipe. Solides compétences en résolution de problèmes et souci du détail. Bonnes compétences communicationnelles et sociales. Bonne connaissance orale du néerlandais et du français, niveau B2 en anglais. Rémunération et package de compensation Nous offrons: CDI ou contrat freelance NL vacature op onze website hier: Clinique Saint-Jean - Kliniek Sint-Jan IT System Engineer (M/V/X) | SmartRecruiters Déclaration d'égalité des chances La Clinique Saint-Jean s'engage à promouvoir la diversité et l'inclusivité dans son environnement de travail.

Il offre aux professionnels des TIC une opportunité unique de participer à la création d’un environnement numérique intégré, ambitieux et orienté vers l’avenir. À propos du rôle Votre mission: L'ingénieur système informatique assure le support et la maintenance de l'infrastructure serveur, des environnements de travail des utilisateurs finaux et effectue des tâches dans le domaine des réseaux. Ce poste requiert une personne proactive dotée d'une solide expérience technique, d'excellentes compétences en matière de résolution de problèmes et d'une grande soif d'apprendre. Responsabilités Gestion et maintenance des environnements Windows Server (Active Directory, stratégie de groupe, DNS, DHCP). Gestion des plateformes de virtualisation VMware (vSphere, ESXi). Mise en œuvre et surveillance des solutions de sauvegarde et de reprise après sinistre à l'aide de Veeam Backup & Replication. Configuration et maintenance de Microsoft System Center Configuration Management (SCCM, Intune). Veiller au respect des meilleures pratiques en matière de sécurité dans tous les systèmes. Fournir une assistance pour Microsoft Intune pour la gestion des appareils et la sécurité des terminaux. Résolution de problèmes liés à des infrastructures complexes et assistance de 2e/3e niveau. Participation à des projets d'infrastructure et contribution aux décisions architecturales. Qualifications Master en technologies de l'information, en informatique ou équivalent grâce à une expérience pertinente. Minimum 3 à 5 ans d'expérience en ingénierie des systèmes informatiques. Connaissance approfondie de : l'administration de Windows Server, la virtualisation VMware, les solutions de sauvegarde Veeam, la suite Microsoft System Center. Connaissance de : la sécurité et la conformité des terminaux, Microsoft Intune et la gestion moderne des appareils. Compréhension des principes fondamentaux des réseaux et des technologies de stockage. Expérience en script PowerShell pour l'automatisation, connaissance des plateformes cloud (Azure, AWS) ou certifications telles que ITIL, MCSA, VMware VCP sont un atout. Capacité à travailler de manière autonome et en équipe. Solides compétences en résolution de problèmes et souci du détail. Bonnes compétences communicationnelles et sociales. Bonne connaissance orale du néerlandais et du français, niveau B2 en anglais. Rémunération et package de compensation Nous offrons: CDI ou contrat freelance NL vacature op onze website hier: Clinique Saint-Jean - Kliniek Sint-Jan IT System Engineer (M/V/X) | SmartRecruiters Déclaration d'égalité des chances La Clinique Saint-Jean s'engage à promouvoir la diversité et l'inclusivité dans son environnement de travail.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5619870368.scraped: About Us At HydroVolta, we're providing sustainable water desalination solutions and technologies for both drinking water and industrial water recycling, all powered by our innovative and patented technologies. The Role We are looking for a skilled and motivated Automation Engineer to join our team! In this role, you will be the brain behind our containerized water treatment plants. Your primary task will be to design, program, and implement the control systems that manage our processes, including the PLCs, pumps, valves, and monitoring equipment. You'll be crucial in ensuring our systems run efficiently, reliably, and autonomously. The Role We are looking for a skilled and motivated Automation & Systems Engineer to join our team! In this role, you will be the brain behind our containerized water treatment plants. Your primary task will be to design, program, and implement PC-based control systems that manage our processes, from local PLC logic to integrated data dashboards . You'll be crucial in ensuring our systems run efficiently, reliably, and autonomously in a connected world. What You'll Do Beckhoff Ecosystem: Design and program PLC systems from the ground up using TwinCAT 3 , utilizing Structured Text (ST) and Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) principles. Smart Dashboards: Build modern, responsive HMI interfaces (using TwinCAT HMI / HTML5) for both local operation and remote monitoring. IIoT & Cloud: Implement Cloud connectivity (MQTT, OPC UA) to stream process data to remote dashboards and internal databases. Process Control: Develop and implement control logic for automated sequences involving pumps, valves, sensors, and other critical components. Hardware Integration: Configure EtherCAT networks and integrate various industrial sensors/instrumentation. Collaborate: Work closely with mechanical and chemical engineering teams to ensure seamless system integration and process optimization. Commissioning: Conduct testing of new systems, both in-house and potentially on-site. What We're Looking For Job-Related Competencies: TwinCAT Mastery: Proven experience with Beckhoff TwinCAT 3 and the Visual Studio environment. Web-Tech Savvy: Familiarity with HTML5, CSS, or JavaScript (highly preferred for HMI development). Connectivity: Solid understanding of IoT protocols (MQTT, AMQP) and industrial communication (Modbus, EtherCAT). Electrical Literacy: Ability to read and interpret P&IDs and electrical schematics (EPLAN experience is a plus). Process Background: Experience with water treatment or chemical process control is a significant advantage. Personal Competencies: Hybrid Mindset: You bridge the gap between "Industrial Electrician" and "Software Developer." Analytical & Problem-Solving: You can troubleshoot complex code and hardware issues logically. Independent: You are self-motivated and can take ownership of the entire automation stack. Team Spirit: You communicate well and enjoy collaborating in a dynamic R&D environment.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5593204044.scraped: Functional / Technical Analyst – Mobile Applications For one of our clients in the financial services sector, we are looking for a Functional / Technical Analyst to join a Mobile DevOps team working on the continuous development of a mobile banking application. Your role As part of an agile DevOps team focused on mobile solutions, you will play a key role in bridging business needs and technical implementation. Your responsibilities include: Analyzing business requirements and translating them into clear functional and technical specifications for mobile applications. Designing and modeling data structures, API interfaces, and object models using UML, ensuring scalability and architectural consistency. Supporting the development process through technical documentation, API specifications (Swagger), and database schema definitions within Oracle environments. Creating wireframes and GUI designs in collaboration with UX designers and front-end developers to deliver intuitive, user-friendly interfaces. Coordinating testing activities by defining test plans, scenarios, acceptance criteria, and supporting functional and technical test execution. Managing and guiding developers within a Scrum framework, acting as owner of technical user stories and ensuring clarity on priorities and requirements. Facilitating communication with stakeholders through refinement sessions, demos, and retrospectives, translating technical topics into business-friendly language. Monitoring progress and quality using sprint metrics, identifying risks, and proposing improvements within DevOps practices. Contributing to the continuous improvement of development processes, tools, and methodologies. Requirements BenefitsWhat’s on offer A supportive and professional working environment that values autonomy, personal growth, and collaboration. Extensive training opportunities, including technical skills, domain knowledge, languages, and personal development. Flexible working hours and the possibility of remote work to support a healthy work-life balance. A competitive salary package with additional benefits such as mobility budget meal vouchers, eco-vouchers, insurance coverage, and flexible benefits. A permanent contract.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5589144928.scraped: Stellantis e-Transmissions, a member of the Stellantis group, operates in Belgium and the Netherlands, specializing in hybrid transmission manufacturing. With a workforce of over 850 employees, robust R&D with state-of-the-art test facilities, and comprehensive operational capabilities, we are at the forefront of the automotive industry. Our product portfolio includes a range of innovative dual clutch hybrid and conventional transmissions, designed to be affordable and deliver superior driving performance, fuel efficiency, and reduced CO2 emissions. We are currently seeking a Design Release Engineer to join our team. By joining us, you'll be part of a leading company that is shaping the future of hybrid transmission technology. We are looking for a highly motivated Design Release engineer to join our Hydraulics team. In this role, you will be the component owner, focusing on MHEV and PHEV. Note that the position is as well open for a junior or low experience engineer to be trained in the role. Responsibilities designing, developing and releasing hydraulic components for Electric Vehicle (EV), Hybrid Electric Vehicle (HEV), and Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV) vehicle applications. Guide the engineering development of hydraulic components from Design Concept into Product Launch, including FE Simulations, Mechanical Design, and Thermal/Flow Analysis. Coordinate the release of designs, obtain design approvals, ensure designs are manufacturable, component readiness, track piece cost, investment & mass, support future design direction, lead and facilitate resolution of component issues Lead design changes through Product Development (PD), Change Request (CRs), Change Notices (CNs), Global Issue management (GIMs), Forever Request (FR), Interim Approval Authorization (IAA), and quality gates (APQP) processes, warranty process Support supplier quality assurance & warranty resolution. Qualifications Bachelor degree in Mechanical, Mechatronics or a closely related engineering degree. Self-starter with a knack for leadership, communication skills, and demonstrated problem solving ability. Understanding engineering theory and principles of design. Ability to work in a team environment. Preferred skills: Advanced degree in engineering. 1+ years of related engineering experience. 1+ years of design release experience. Understanding of Hydraulics, design, manufacturing, testing & development Experience with Design Failure Mode & Effects Analysis (DFMEA). Knowledge of drawing GD&T and dimensional tolerance stack-ups. Experience with Statement of Requirements, DFMEA, and Design Reviews. Working knowledge of problem resolution and tracking systems and engineering change management processes. Design for Six Sigma (DFSS) Certification. We offer You will join an enthusiastic team and a respected company known for its strong international focus in the challenging automotive industry We offer a dynamic work environment with opportunities for growth and development and exposure to new best in class simulation tools and new technologies You can count on motivated colleagues in a professional setting that values creativity, initiative and effective communication We provide a congestion-free access and an attractive salary package with all kinds of fringe benefits

WHEN YOU JOIN US, YOU WILL BE. Closely adheres to defined Global IT Service Management processes (when published) – change management, incident management, problem management, service level management, knowledge management and request fulfillment processes Partners with IT staff, service providers, and end-users to support and maintain IKO’s complex and quickly growing core infrastructure. Provide level 2 or 3 support, and above, for any global core infrastructure issue in line with defined SLA’s and OLA’s. Hands over unresolved critical/high priority incidents to other global team members with detailed supporting documentation at the end of normal working hours or under coordination of Manager if for an off-hours incident. Scheduled for support in on-call rotation and, for the period scheduled, is expected to respond (within SLA) and troubleshoot incidents for any global location outside of business-hours. For example, during the night and at weekends. Responsible for the uptime, resiliency and performance of all core infrastructure with a focus on ERP systems including Oracle JD Edwards Help manage, optimize and plan capacity for IKO’s entire core ERP infrastructure including storage environments. Creation and maintenance of published documentation for technical procedures, policies, and standard processes related to core infrastructure Collaborates with project team members, providing technical expertise, assist in creation of detailed task plans and executes activities, communicating status accordingly Research new technologies to stay informed on core infrastructure industry standards and best practices. Makes recommendations to Manager, Global Core Infrastructure for inclusion in technology roadmap Implement, administer, and monitor security solutions, standards, and policies in accordance with IKO’s Information Security Framework. Investigates possible security breaches as part of incident management team Additional duties as assigned by the Manager, Global Core Infrastructure OUR IDEAL CANDIDATE. Bachelor's degree or equivalent in Information Technology, Computer Science, Engineering or related field. 5-10 years’ experience managing and maintaining datacenter systems, processes and systems architecture in large enterprises, ideally in support of ERP systems Effective leader of ideas and people with a deep understanding of business capabilities and processes Broad industry experience and domain expertise –industrial manufacturing sector experience an asset Ability to assess datacenter trends in order to develop and implement hardware and software standards for network Collaborative team player who can effectively participate in departmental level meetings and positively influence outcomes Solid understanding of licensing models and practice, enterprise inventory tools, all to ensure compliance is maintained. Oracle license experience beneficial. Upholds 'best practice' standards as well as departmental policies and procedures Excellent communication and presentations skills Excellent problem-solving skill Able to travel globally to supported locations (up to 25%) Additional Key Skills/Experience: Oracle Enterprise Linux, Red Hat Enterprise Linux (5.x, 6.x, 7.x) Oracle JD Edwards, WebLogic, WebCenter, DB experience an asset VMWare (ESX 6.x) in an large enterprise multi-site environment in support of Oracle ERP/DB Windows server 2012, 2016, 2019 NFS/NAS/Distributed File Shares Virtual machine and Oracle DB Backup methods and functions (CommVault knowledge an asset) Microsoft Active Directory Management Enterprise Anti-Virus HP Server Management (Proliant/C7000/Synergy/OneView) Enterprise System Monitoring Ticket entry and change management experience #LI-SM2

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5607116360.scraped: The Job As a Data Platform Engineer, you are the backbone of data ecosystems. You create scalable, resilient platforms that empower data engineers, analysts, and scientists to turn raw data into meaningful insights fast, safely and with confidence. You take ownership of the underlying infrastructure, tooling, and standards that make data work at scale, balancing performance, cost and reliability. From cloud resources and orchestration tot data governance and observability. You ensure the platform is robust today and ready for tomorrow’s use cases, enabling teams to focus on creating value from data rather than managing complexity. At Dataroots , our mission is to innovate, design, and deliver robust data solutions and platforms for a variety of sectors. Our growing team operates out of Leuven and Ghent, where our dedicated in-house data & AI specialists apply state-of-the-art methodologies to optimize and transform our clients' business processes. Our strong emphasis on DataOps and MLOps means we build solutions that are not only intelligent but also production-ready, offering clients a solid foundation to make data-driven decisions with confidence. . What you can expect at Dataroots? Dataroots was founded in 2016 and, since its beginnings, has focused on helping its customers achieve excellence in their data-driven operations. The only way to do this sustainably is by investing in team well-being, diversity, knowledge sharing and customer experience. This quality-first focus has allowed sustained and healthy growth over the years. Join a team where you can have an impact on our growth story. Read up more on our DNA over at [https://dataroots.io/our-dna]. The Skills Data Platform And Cloud Engineering skills: Expertise in data engineering and cloud architecture. Proficiency in building ELT data pipelines using tools like Airflow, Prefect, Dagster, Spark, dbt, or related tooling. Mastery of software engineering skills including Git, Python, and SQL. Expertise in cloud engineering with tooling like Terraform, Docker, Kubernetes, … for robust and scalable cloud infrastructure. Understanding of at least one data platform such as Databricks, Snowflake, Fabric, Azure data services, or AWS data services. The Offer An attractive salary with extralegal benefits, including: Mobility budget or a company car with fuel/charging card Hospitalization and group insurance High-end laptop Smartphone with subscription Substantial amount of holidays Meal vouchers ... Diverse and welcoming work environment , where you’ll collaborate & unwind with colleagues from different cultures and disciplines. The organization of both fun & professional events and initiatives is actively encouraged and supported. A training budget for individual and team learning opportunities. Tons of team-building events and sports initiatives to stay connected and unwind. Where you’ll work Our main offices are in Leuven and Ghent , with co-working spaces in Charleroi and Antwerp . These spaces are perfect for both focusing and unwinding with colleagues, as well as brainstorming sessions and team events. While much of your work can be performed remotely, you will also work on-site with clients. Ready to join Dataroots? Excited to shape the future as a Data Platform Engineer with us? We’re looking forward to hearing from you! Apply now and start a new chapter with Dataroots!

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5614812723.scraped: Job Description At TOMRA Food, the Systems & Sensors team takes ownership of how all components in our machines work together. In addition, we focus on the quality of inspection by designing cutting-edge vision systems to unlock the ultimate sorting capability, ensure quality, minimise waste and maximise customer value. Together with the other teams in R&D, we are proud to own the complexity and deliver the breakthrough. Your mission: As a Systems Engineer , in our office in Leuven , you are the technical linchpin that connects innovative R&D concepts to robust, market-leading machine performance. You will: Take the technical lead in the end-to-end design, configuration, installation and testing of our roadmap sorting machines. Innovate vision systems: Actively support the research and development of optical systems and sensor technologies, pushing boundaries of inspection quality. Drive optimisation: utilise your hands on approach to build test-setups, rigorously collect and analyse data and document findings to drive machine improvements. Complete technical documentation: Develop and maintain documentation for system architecture, configuration and operational procedures. Have cross-functional impact: Collaborate across a vast technical landscape including optics, automation, production, supply chain and service. Provide technical guidance and demonstrate leadership.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5611767270.scraped: Description de l'emploi Nous recrutons un.e Automation Engineer pour une entreprise de la région de Vielsalm. En tant qu’Automation Engineer, vous êtes responsable du suivi du parc d’automates et du réseau de communication, en veillant au respect des standards de sécurité, de la culture « Zero Harm », de la qualité et des normes environnementales. Votre mission principale est d’assurer la fiabilité et l’optimisation des systèmes automatisés tout en apportant un support technique aux équipes. Vos responsabilités : - Identifier les besoins d’amélioration pour maintenir ou accroître la fiabilité des systèmes. - Analyser et résoudre les dysfonctionnements techniques. - Optimiser les interfaces HMI en fonction des besoins de la production et des erreurs détectées. - Participer aux projets d’installation de nouveaux équipements : validation technique et intégration dans le réseau existant. - Développer et tester les interfaces machines pour intégrer les nouveaux équipements. - Définir et assurer le suivi des règles de back-up et des pontages. - Investiguer les sources d’amélioration et proposer des solutions innovantes. - Gérer les stocks de pièces de rechange (cartes mémoires…) et garantir leur disponibilité. - Former le personnel dans votre domaine de compétences. - Être l’interlocuteur privilégié du service IT. - Assurer une communication claire et fiable avec la hiérarchie, les collègues, les techniciens et les opérateurs.Profil- Diplôme minimum : Bachelier en automation avec une expérience confirmée en milieu industriel. - Expertise technique en électricité et automation. - Maîtrise des systèmes Siemens (S7 400, S7 1500, Simatic Manager, TIA Portal). - Connaissance des protocoles Profibus, Profinet, communication inter-PLC. - Expérience avec Scada WinCC ou Wonderware. - Atouts supplémentaires : PLC Beckhoff TwinCat, variateurs ABB et SEW, matériel Safety, S5, programmation List. - Bonnes capacités de communication, sens des responsabilités, autonomie. - Excellentes compétences d’analyse, résolution de problèmes et esprit d’initiative. - Maîtrise des outils informatiques (Suite Office, ERP SAP). - Connaissance linguistique : anglais requis.OffreNotre client vous propose : - Un contrat à durée indéterminée dans un environnement industriel innovant. - Un salaire compétitif complété par des avantages extra-légaux. - La possibilité de leasing vélo. - Des formations pour soutenir votre développement professionnel. - De réelles perspectives d’évolution de carrière. - Une stratégie claire en matière de durabilité (pour la planète, les clients et les collaborateurs). - Des réductions intéressantes sur les produits de l’entreprise. - Des avantages exclusifs via la plateforme benefits@work. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5611216560.scraped: We're working with a gaming company based in Brussels who are looking for an Infrastructure Engineer to help build and evolve the systems that keep their games online and their teams moving fast. This role is heavily involved in the technical platform: supporting cloud and On-Prem infrastructure, improving automation, and making sure environments are secure. It's hands-on, varied, and ideal for someone who enjoys both building and maintaining systems. Responsibilities Designing, deploying, and maintaining cloud and On-Prem infrastructure Managing AWS environments, focusing on core compute and Storage services Operating and improving virtualized and containerized platforms (ideally Proxmox) Maintaining and supporting Linux (Debian-based) and Windows systems, including server and Embedded environments Building and maintaining infrastructure using TerraForm and Ansible Writing and maintaining scripts in Python, Bash, and/or PowerShell to automate operations Supporting and improving CI/CD pipelines, with a solid understanding of how delivery workflows function Troubleshooting infrastructure issues and improving system reliability and performance Collaborating closely with development and technical teams to support game platforms Applying basic networking knowledge to diagnose and resolve connectivity or performance issues Your profile AWS, with solid knowledge of classic compute and Storage services Virtualization and containers, ideally with Proxmox Linux environments, preferably Debian-based (server and Embedded applications) Windows systems, including server and Embedded use cases Infrastructure as Code & automation using TerraForm and Ansible Scripting skills in Python and/or Bash (PowerShell is a plus) CI/CD concepts, with a good understanding of how pipelines work Basic networking knowledge is a plus The offer A chance to work in the gaming industry, where performance and uptime really matter A mix of cloud and On-Prem infrastructure, not just one or the other Real ownership over systems and automation, not just maintenance A collaborative technical team that values practical solutions over buzzwords Flexible hybrid work Up to EUR6.500 (dependent on experience & interview process) Full package including mobility options/car

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5608563120.scraped: At Capgemini Engineering, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world's most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous cars to life-saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities. Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same. Your role As an Automation Engineer , you will play a pivotal role in designing, implementing, and optimizing industrial automation systems with a focus on data integration and analytics, particularly in GMP-regulated environments. In this role you will play a key role in: Designing and implementing PLC/SCADA systems and industrial networks (Modbus, Profinet, Profibus, Ethernet/IP) for manufacturing clients in pharmaceutical and other regulated industries. Developing GMP-compliant automation solutions including programming, validation, and documentation that meet strict regulatory requirements. Creating and optimizing data historian systems (WinCC, Ignition, OSIsoft PI) to enable data-driven decision making and process improvement. Implementing Industrial IoT architectures using OPC UA, MQTT, and edge computing technologies to connect shop floor systems with enterprise applications. Supporting FAT/SAT execution, validation activities, and troubleshooting for complex automation projects. Collaborating with cross-functional teams including production, quality, and IT to deliver integrated automation solutions. Your profile Bachelor's or Master's degree in Automation, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering or related field. 7+ years of experience in industrial automation with focus on data systems in regulated environments. Strong expertise in PLC programming (preferably Siemens TIA Portal) and HMI/SCADA development (WinCC). Proven experience with industrial communication protocols and networking (Profinet, Profibus, Modbus, Ethernet/IP). Hands-on experience implementing data historian systems and industrial IoT solutions. Knowledge of GMP compliance requirements and validation processes for automated systems. Fluent in English and French. What you'll love about working here Free access to learning platforms: Free access for all to world-class learning assets and curated programs from Harvard Business Review, Coursera, Pluralsight, Udemy, Microsoft, AWS, Google and many more. Communities: Our Framework drives career development and elevates Capgemini's capabilities across various domains. Roles like architects, software engineers, these communities form the pillars of our Engineering Career Framework, outlining the foundational elements and anticipated growth. Worldwide Leader of Engineering Services: Capgemini Engineering combines its broad industry knowledge and cutting-edge technologies in digital and software to support the convergence of the physical and digital worlds. We help our clients unleashing their R&D potential to develop smart products and services of tomorrow. About Capgemini Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fuelled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.

Responsibilities Designing, deploying, and maintaining cloud and on-prem infrastructure Managing AWS environments, focusing on core compute and storage services Operating and improving virtualized and containerized platforms (ideally Proxmox) Maintaining and supporting Linux (Debian-based) and Windows systems, including server and embedded environments Building and maintaining infrastructure using Terraform and Ansible Writing and maintaining scripts in Python, Bash, and/or PowerShell to automate operations Supporting and improving CI/CD pipelines, with a solid understanding of how delivery workflows function Troubleshooting infrastructure issues and improving system reliability and performance Collaborating closely with development and technical teams to support game platforms Applying basic networking knowledge to diagnose and resolve connectivity or performance issues Your Profile AWS, with solid knowledge of classic compute and storage services Virtualization and containers, ideally with Proxmox Linux environments, preferably Debian-based (server and embedded applications) Windows systems, including server and embedded use cases Infrastructure as Code & automation using Terraform and Ansible Scripting skills in Python and/or Bash (PowerShell is a plus) CI/CD concepts, with a good understanding of how pipelines work Basic networking knowledge is a plus The Offer A chance to work in the gaming industry, where performance and uptime really matter A mix of cloud and on-prem infrastructure, not just one or the other Real ownership over systems and automation, not just maintenance A collaborative technical team that values practical solutions over buzzwords Flexible hybrid work Up to €6.500 (dependent on experience & interview process) Full package including mobility options/car

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5580512942.scraped: 360dialog is the leading Whatsapp platform for Independent Software Providers. We are hosting the Whatsapp channels for more than 800 software solutions and 45000+ B2B clients. As a bootstrapped and profitable company with no investors in the cap table, we are free to do what makes sense from the business as well as from the tech perspective. Our mission is to enable businesses to not only adapt messaging channels but also use them for performance use cases and drive revenue through them. We love to use state of the art engineering methods all over the place, in software development, tech operations but you will find them also in non-tech areas through the whole organization. We are looking for like-minded people to join our organization and contribute with their ideas, skills and expertise in exchange for a good payment. What we offer: Here’s an opportunity to shape a whole new industry in Business Messaging. You’ll be joining a team of experienced entrepreneurs who run a bootstrapped and profitable global company. Join a dynamic, innovative team where you'll collaborate with some of the brightest minds in tech. Enjoy daily opportunities to learn, grow, and push the boundaries of what's possible. Work from anywhere in the world with full remote flexibility. Receive a competitive salary package that reflects your skills, experience and achievements. NB: This is a contract-based position. We are a fully remote company and welcome applicants from anywhere in the world—please disregard the listed countries, which are included only due to system requirements. Requirements Key Requirements 4+ years in DevOps / SRE / Cloud Engineering. Proactive, independent ownership — you anticipate issues, make clear trade-offs, and drive outcomes without hand-holding. Deep Terraform experience (modules, tests, structure, scalability). Production GitOps (ArgoCD strongly preferred). Solid Kubernetes (GKE ideal; multi-cluster a plus). Modern CI/CD experience (any tool). Hands-on with Vault, Prometheus, Grafana . Ability to code for infra (Python, or Go, Bash). Fluent English, growth mindset , and hunger to learn. Budget The budget range for this role is €5.000,00/Month – €10.000,00/Month, depending on the candidate’s level of experience and fit with the position requirements. Background Check Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge and agree that 360Dialog may conduct a background check as part of the recruitment and selection process. This may include, verification of your professional experience, educational background, and other relevant information necessary to assess your suitability for the role.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5605572885.scraped: About the Company: Haulogy is on a mission to digitalize the energy sector and speed up the green energy transition while lowering energy costs for everyone. We are successfully impacting this transition today by providing energy and utility market players with a modular and scalable management software and relevant business and IT services in a long-term partnership approach. This position is available within our New Solutions department, which focuses on creating innovative solutions that power the energy transition. We are a team of experts dedicated to building cutting-edge products that address the evolving needs of the energy market, fostering a culture of innovation , efficiency , and deep business expertise . The Product and Team: Our department develops and manages a portfolio of advanced software solutions designed to harvest and optimize the flexibility offered by renewable production and consumption in energy applications: Flex.Guard: A platform that enables electricity grid operators to dynamically manage network constraints and increase the grid's capacity to host renewable energy sources. Flex.Boost: An AI-based software that controls flexible energy processes (like battery storage, industrial loads, and EV charging) to optimize energy costs against volatile market prices and maximize self-consumption. Our teams are organized into empowered, autonomous "squads" . Each squad is a cross-functional unit that owns specific business domains, allowing them to develop a deep understanding of customer needs and deliver value in short, rapid iterations. You will join one of these squads as a key engineering contributor. The Role: We’re looking for a Software or Applied Math Engineer with a strong background in Power Systems who wants to work at the intersection of electricity networks, energy optimization, and real-world software products. In this role, you will turn complex energy system models and algorithms into production-ready software that supports electricity grid operations and energy optimization that make a real impact on the energy transition. Responsibilities: As a Power System Software Engineer, you will be a key member of our development team, combining technical expertise with sound engineering practices to build software that delivers real value to users. Energy System Modeling: Design and implement algorithms for electricity grid management, with a strong focus on network constraints, capacity optimization, and system reliability. Production-Ready Code Development: Translate advanced power system concepts into software implementation that operates at scale in the field. Team Collaboration: Work within a cross-functional squad (engineering, product, energy experts) to deliver value in fast, iterative cycles. Qualifications: To excel in this role, you should possess the following qualifications: Educational Background: A Master’s degree or PhD in Applied Mathematics, Electrical Engineering, Power Systems, or a related field with a strong focus on IT/Software. Domain Knowledge: Deep understanding of electrical grid operations and optimization. Technical Expertise: Proficiency in Python is required. Ability and desire to learn new technologies, programming languages and best coding practices. Collaboration Tools: Familiarity with collaborative software development practices using Git/GitHub or similar platforms. Problem-Solving Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to tackle complex technical challenges. Additional Desired Qualifications: Comfortable engaging with product and domain stakeholders to ensure technical solutions address real customer challenges. Professional proficiency in English. Fluency in French is a plus.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5599589212.scraped: Are you passionate about cybersecurity and cloud technologies? Do you thrive on solving complex challenges and building secure, automated solutions? We are looking for a Cloud Security Engineer to help strengthen our growing Cloud SOC team. As part of our Managed SOC offering, we deliver a full-service Security Operations Center to customers. In this engineering role, you’ll go beyond monitoring—you’ll design, automate, and optimize security services that make a real impact. As a Cloud Security Engineer, you will: Set up and configure new customers in our SOC environment. Build workflows and integrations to streamline security operations. Optimize detection rules and create advanced threat detection logic. Explore and implement AI-driven enhancements for security processes. Contribute to new services like Vulnerability Management as a Service (VMaaS) and External Attack Surface Management (EASM). Share ideas, suggest improvements, and help shape the future of our SOC. We’re looking for someone with: A degree in IT and ideally 1–2 years of experience in infrastructure or security engineering. Familiarity with Microsoft technologies and cloud security concepts. Automation & Infrastructure: Logic Apps, Terraform, Azure Security Tools: Microsoft Sentinel, Defender XDR Scripting: Python, PowerShell, Bash Strong problem-solving skills, eager to learn and contribute. Fluent in Dutch & English, French is a plus. You are a true team player who values knowledge sharing and collective success. At SecWise, we believe that security is serious business—but work should still be fun. We’re a tight‑knit team within the Cronos Group, and as a proud member of the Microsoft Intelligent Security Association (MISA), we stay at the forefront of modern security innovation. Knowledge sharing, personal growth, and a healthy work‑life balance are part of our DNA. Here’s what we offer: A competitive salary package with a company car. Great benefits through the Cronos Group care package. A flat, open culture where your ideas matter. Support to grow into the best version of yourself. Ready to engineer the future of cloud security? Apply now or reach out to learn more about how you can make an impact at SecWise. Download attachment(s) This job as a PDF file

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5598849535.scraped: As an Integration and Automation Engineer, you'll be the technical powerhouse behind our client projects. Your responsibility will be to design, build and maintain integrations that enable enterprise systems to communicate efficiently, securely, and at scale. You'll also collaborate with multiple stakeholders to streamline and automate business processes. What drives you? We're seeking a community-oriented engineer who excels at solving complex integration problems, collaborating with colleagues, and exchanging knowledge. You have a genuine passion for technology and innovation, and you turn that energy into meaningful results for clients. You thrive on advising clients, steering them through technical decisions and implementations. What do you bring to the table? Problem-solving mindset with excellent communication and collaboration skills. Experience with leading integration/iPaaS platforms Workato, Boomi, MuleSoft, etc.). In-depth knowledge of APIs REST, SOAP, GraphQL, etc.), messaging technologies JMS, AMQP, Kafka, etc.), and other integration patterns. Track record integrating large enterprise IT systems SAP, Dynamics, NetSuite, Salesforce, etc.). Experience in one or more cloud platforms Azure, AWS, or GCP. Fluent in English. (Dutch and/or French is a plus). Nice to have: Experience with Kubernetes and DevOps Technologies. Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions. Experience in business process modeling and automation. What will you be doing? You primary focus will be designing and implementing robust integration solutions, but your role extends beyond coding. You will also work closely with business stakeholders to align on process requirements and design business automation solutions. Design, develop, and test complex integration scenarios using leading iPaaS platforms and resilient integration patterns. Model and optimize business processes , working with business analysts to translate requirements into automated workflows. Lead integration-centered projects and architectures , including API Management, Data Integration, Application Integration, EDA, and B2B Integration. Work with a diverse range of clients , including public sector organizations and international enterprises. Always taking a pragmatic approach. Grow as an expert by focusing on knowledge, absorbing the experience from peers, and actively contributing to the i8c community. What do we offer? Become part of the biggest integration community in Belgium : you'll find a group of like-minded integration experts, each with their own areas of interest and expertise. From Event-driven Architecture and API Management to security and DevOps, we all share a passion for knowledge sharing and technical discussions. You're in the driver's seat of your career path : your career is in your hands. Whether you aspire to be an entrepreneur or a specialist, we are here to support your ambitions. Continuous learning opportunities : you get the opportunity to collaborate on innovative initiatives and expand your knowledge, and we provide access to training to guide you on your path. Enjoy the freedom to work where you're most productive : our team operates in a hybrid mode, splitting time between the customer's sites, our offices in Kontich, Ghent, and at home. Great remuneration package : you can count on a competitive salary along with numerous extra-legal benefits (company car, fuel card, laptop, phone, mobile subscription, insurance,...)

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5598849231.scraped: Why we approach Applied Observability from within an integration point of view? Simple. Integration is where context lives. Integration stitches APIs, events, data contracts and workflows into one living system. Start observability here and you see end-to-end truth instead of isolated noise . In a landscape where AI, automation, composable architectures and quantum computing constantly redraw the playing field, that context makes all the difference. It enables organizations not just to react to what happens, but to anticipate what comes next . We strongly believe Applied Observability is the strategic lens for foresight. Together with specialists like you, we bring it to life aided with our integration background to help organizations not only keep pace with disruption, but stay ahead of it. WHO ARE WE? Hi, we are Archers! Nice to meet you. We’re a community of 25 integration architects and analysts within CornerBriX , Belgium’s leading integration community. It’s quite simple: if you love sharing knowledge, having your insights challenged, and experimenting with technology, you’ll feel right at home with us. You’ll join a community that challenges existing digital systems. Dares to ask the right questions. That loves to think critically about technological innovations. Our Archers Knowledge Days (AKDs) are the perfect example: community days dedicated to exchanging lessons from projects, conferences and new tech so you can level up fast. WHAT DOES YOUR JOB AS AN APPLIED OBSERVABILITY SPECIALIST AT ARCHERS LOOK LIKE You'll drive observability initiatives together with our community of integration experts by implementing the Archers Observability Program for leading enterprises. You’ll design intelligent alerting and visualization mechanism that deliver real business value by capturing traces, logs and metrics from integrated systems. You’ll analyze opportunities across organizational layers to strenghten observability capabilities and prepare systems for the AId)riven future. You'll design and implement monitoring and troubleshooting solutions that ensure seamless integrations to underpin resilient system landscapes. You'll collaborate with architects, developers and operations to embed observability best practices across cross-functional teams. You'll mentor and coach customers in the Archers methodology and frameworks through seminars, training and direct guidance so they can sustain and evolve observability maturity. WHAT WE EXPECT FROM APPLIED OBSERVABILITY SPECIALIST You are familiar with one or more observability and logging & monitoring technologies like Grafana, Prometheus, Dynatrace, Graylog, Datadog, AppDynamics, New Relic, Elastic, Splunk, Instana,... You have hands-on experience with the architecture and design of integrations, services, APIs, etc. You have experience with architecture/design modeling languages (such as Archimate and UML) and tools such as Sparx Enterprise Architect or Archi. You have experience with one or more interface technologies and concepts : XSD, WSDL, SOAP, REST, Swagger, OpenAPI, JSON, and others. WHAT WE OFFER OUR APPLIED OBSERVABILITY SPECIALIST Be part of the leading integration community: As part of CornerBriX , you'll join Belgium's leading integration community , certified as both a Great Place to Work and a Best Workplace . You'll connect with more than 180 experts, with knowledge ranging from business and data architecture to platform engineering, iPaaS, cloud-native integration platfroms and much more. Sustainability and wellbeing : We care about people and the world around us. From health to environment and society, we act with impact guided by the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Continuous learning : Your growth and job happiness come first. We tackle challenging projects together, challenge each other's thinking, discuss emerging technologies, and help you become as happy as you can be in your career . Room to experiment and innovate : We stay ahead by giving you space to explore what's new . You'll always have the time and tools to sharpen your skills, try out new technologies, and lead in a fast-moving world. Whenever you need it. Hybrid working : Flexibility is part of how we work, whether you're collaborating at a customers site, connecting form our offices in Kontich or Ghent, or working from home in line with customer policies. Competitive package : Count on a competitive remuneration package including a company car, charging pass, laptop, phone, subscription and insurance.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5598848774.scraped: Are you passionate about cybersecurity and cloud technologies? Do you thrive on solving complex challenges and building secure, automated solutions? At SecWise , a proud member of the Microsoft Intelligent Security Association (MISA), we’re looking for a Cloud Security Engineer to strengthen our growing Cloud SOC team in Leuven. As part of our Managed SOC offering, we deliver a full-service Security Operations Center to our customers. In this engineering role, you’ll go beyond monitoring—you’ll design, automate, and optimize security services that make a real impact. As a Cloud Security Engineer, you will: Set up and configure new customers in our SOC environment. Build workflows and integrations to streamline security operations. Optimize detection rules and create advanced threat detection logic. Explore and implement AI-driven enhancements for security processes. Contribute to new services like Vulnerability Management as a Service (VMaaS) and External Attack Surface Management (EASM). Share ideas, suggest improvements, and help shape the future of our SOC. We’re looking for someone with: A degree in IT and ideally 1–2 years of experience in infrastructure or security engineering. Familiarity with Microsoft technologies and cloud security concepts. Automation & Infrastructure: Logic Apps, Terraform, Azure Security Tools: Microsoft Sentinel, Defender XDR Scripting: Python, PowerShell, Bash Strong problem-solving skills, eager to learn and contribute. Fluent in Dutch & English, French is a plus. You are a true team player who values knowledge sharing and collective success. At SecWise , we believe that security is serious business—but work should still be fun. We’re a tight-knit team within the Cronos Group, where knowledge sharing, personal growth, and a healthy work-life balance are part of our DNA. Here’s what we offer: A competitive salary package with a company car. Great benefits through the Cronos Group care package. A flat, open culture where your ideas matter. Support to grow into the best version of yourself. Ready to engineer the future of cloud security? Apply now or reach out to learn more about how you can make an impact at SecWise.

What We Offer A key technical role in a modern manufacturing environment. Growth path toward Senior Infrastructure Engineer. Access to advanced trainings and certifications. A supportive, handson engineering culture. Competitive compensation and benefits based on experience and performance.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5591932840.scraped: Your job in a nutshell As Test Automation Engineer, you'll join an enthusiastic multidisciplinary team at our HQ in Brussels to develop qualitative applications in a fast-changing IT landscape. Your role will contribute to innovations benefiting millions of clients, ensuring product and code quality from start to finish through test automation. And in detail As a test automation engineer, you'll be joining an enthusiastic multidisciplinary team. Together, you develop qualitative applications for a fast-changing IT landscape: an exciting and versatile challenge! You monitor product and code quality, from start to finish You help determine the feature test plan and the test scope You focus maximum on automation of test cases You share your test results You identify and correct any sticking points and iron out any bugs Your talents and competences You're the physical embodiment of the Scrum values: involvement, focus, openness and respect, with a good dose of courage to boot You're inquisitive and driven—and you're Always On the ball You thrive in a team environment, happily sharing your knowledge and insights You are quick to make connections and dare to question everything that deserves to be questioned You obtained your bachelor or master degree in Computer Sciences or IT You have a good knowledge of Dutch or French, and great English skills You know your way around Develop and maintain automated test scripts using Java and test automation frameworks (e.g., Selenium, TestNG, JUnit, Cucumber etc.). Create comprehensive test plans, test cases, and test data for both functional and non-functional testing. Execute automated tests, analyze results, and report defects with detailed logs and steps for replication. Integrate test automation into CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipelines. Work with the development team to identify areas for automation and prioritize test cases. Debug and troubleshoot automation scripts and applications in various environments. Ensure the scalability, reliability, and effectiveness of automated test frameworks and tools. Maintain documentation for test automation frameworks, processes, and results. Have knowledge on the standards in software testing (ISTQB) You surprise us with your experience or knowledge of the tools and/or programming languages below Java Selenium Cypress Gherkin JMeter Postman Ruby PHP Jenkins Swagger GitLab Python What we have to offer Sustainability AND impact We strongly believe in the positive impact that the financial sector can have on the world. BNP Paribas Fortis offers sustainable and responsible investment products and solutions and encourages social entrepreneurship. Together with our customers, we are working towards a better future every day. Development & career perspectives The departments that make up BNP Paribas Fortis cover a wide range of activities. You will therefore have many opportunities for "horizontal" and "vertical" career development. Good place TO work In addition to your remuneration and benefits (meal vouchers, insurance, holidays), we offer flexible salary options, allowing you to convert part of your salary into personalised benefits (phone use, IT equipment, transport, etc.). Our engagement At BNP Paribas Fortis, we want to attract and retain all talent, whatever their gender, age, background or sexual orientation, and irrespective of whether they are living with a disability, as every person has their own experiences and their own identity. All of our full-time vacancies are also open to candidates wishing to work on an 80% or 90% full-time equivalent basis. Tempted by the challenge? A first selection is made based on your CV and motivation letter. Are we convinced that you are the right candidate? Then we will contact you. Good luck! Is this position not for you, but do you know someone who is cut out for this job? Feel free to share it with friends and family! Our engagement At BNP Paribas Fortis, we want to attract and retain all talent, whatever their gender, age, background or sexual orientation, and irrespective of whether they are living with a disability, as all persons have their own experiences and their own identity. All of our full-time vacancies are also open to candidates wishing to work on an 80% or 90% full-time equivalent basis. If the function is categorized as a banking services provider function (cf. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5591637094.scraped: Description En tant que Design & Build Engineer, vos missions consisteront à : Concevoir et planifier des projets techniques en respectant les exigences clients et réglementaires ; Participer à la réalisation des études de faisabilité, des designs techniques et des plans d’exécution ; Collaborer avec les équipes de construction et les parties prenantes pour assurer le suivi des projets ; Superviser l’intégration des solutions techniques et veiller à la qualité, aux coûts et aux délais ; Contribuer à l’amélioration continue des processus de conception et de construction. Company Notre client est un acteur majeur du secteur de la construction et des concessions, reconnu pour ses projets d’envergure et son engagement envers l’innovation et la qualité. Présent à l’international, il offre un environnement dynamique où l’expertise technique et la collaboration sont valorisées, et où chaque collaborateur peut contribuer à des projets ambitieux et structurants. Profile Vous êtes le profil idéal pour cette mission de Design & Build Engineer si : Vous êtes titulaire d’un master en ingénierie civile, mécanique, électrique ou dans un domaine similaire ; Vous disposez d’une expérience significative sur des projets d’installation et/ou des projets de type DBFM ; Vous possédez de bonnes connaissances en HVAC ; Vous maîtrisez les logiciels de conception et de modélisation (ex. : AutoCAD, Revit, BIM, logiciels de simulation) ; Vous êtes rigoureux(se), autonome et capable de travailler en équipe multidisciplinaire ; Vous avez un excellent sens de l’organisation et de la communication , et savez gérer plusieurs projets simultanément . Offer Pour cette offre de Design & Build Engineer, notre client vous offre : Un package salarial compétitif et des avantages sociaux attractifs ; La possibilité de contribuer à des projets techniques innovants et d’envergure ; Un environnement de travail stimulant au sein d’un groupe reconnu à l’international ; Des opportunités de développement professionnel et de formation continue.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5590138650.scraped: Chez Anderson, nous accompagnons les PME dans la modernisation, la sécurisation et l’exploitation de leurs environnements IT. En tant qu’Ingénieur·e Système Cloud, tu joues un rôle clé dans la qualité et la fiabilité des environnements IT de nos clients. Tu travailles directement avec des PME pour exploiter, faire évoluer et sécuriser leurs environnements. Ton rôle combine expertise technique, contact client et vision long terme : résoudre des problématiques lorsqu’elles se présentent — du support quotidien L1 et L2 aux incidents complexes L3 — mais surtout construire des environnements modernes, fiables et sécurisés. Tes responsabilités Tu participes activement à la gestion quotidienne, à l’évolution et à la sécurisation des environnements IT de nos clients. Concrètement, tu es amené·e à : Gérer et maintenir les environnements Microsoft 365 et Azure Assurer leur disponibilité, leurs performances et leur sécurité Intervenir directement auprès des clients pour résoudre des incidents techniques complexes et en prévenir la récurrence Participer aux migrations vers Microsoft 365 et Azure (messagerie, identité, fichiers, postes de travail) Déployer et maintenir les fonctionnalités de sécurité Microsoft (Defender, MFA, accès conditionnel) Contribuer aux stratégies de sauvegarde, de reprise et de résilience Collaborer avec les autres ingénieur·e·s pour assurer la continuité de service Documenter les environnements, procédures et bonnes pratiques Identifier des opportunités d’amélioration pour les clients et pour Anderson Qui tu es Bachelier ou master en informatique (ou équivalent par l’expérience) 2 à 3 ans d’expérience dans des environnements systèmes et/ou cloud À l’aise dans le contact direct avec les clients À l’aise aussi bien dans le support utilisateur que dans des tâches d’ingénierie systèmes Autonome, structuré·e et responsable En veille technologique continue, avec l’envie d’apprendre Ton environnement technique Microsoft 365 (Exchange, SharePoint, Teams), Azure & Entra ID Intune et les concepts de Modern Workplace Virtualisation (Hyper-V, VMware) Sauvegarde & reprise (Veeam) Notions solides en sécurité (identité, protection des endpoints, contrôle des accès) Tu ne dois pas tout maîtriser dès le premier jour : nous privilégions l’état d’esprit, la capacité d’analyse et l’envie d’évoluer. Pourquoi rejoindre Anderson ? Un impact réel sur les clients et les solutions mises en place Des projets Cloud & Sécurité modernes Un rôle qui combine expertise technique et relation client Des perspectives d’évolution selon ton profil et tes ambitions Ce que nous offrons Package salarial compétitif Voiture de société et carte carburant Chèques-repas, éco-chèques, primes Télétravail avec indemnité forfaitaire Assurance groupe et hospitalisation (couverture familiale) GSM et abonnement Budget formation & certifications Une équipe engagée et technique

Concrètement, tu es amené·e à : Gérer et maintenir les environnements Microsoft 365 et Azure Assurer leur disponibilité, leurs performances et leur sécurité Intervenir directement auprès des clients pour résoudre des incidents techniques complexes et en prévenir la récurrence Participer aux migrations vers Microsoft 365 et Azure (messagerie, identité, fichiers, postes de travail) Déployer et maintenir les fonctionnalités de sécurité Microsoft (Defender, MFA, accès conditionnel) Contribuer aux stratégies de sauvegarde, de reprise et de résilience Collaborer avec les autres ingénieur·e·s pour assurer la continuité de service Documenter les environnements, procédures et bonnes pratiques Identifier des opportunités d’amélioration pour les clients et pour Anderson Qui tu es Bachelier ou master en informatique (ou équivalent par l’expérience) 2 à 3 ans d’expérience dans des environnements systèmes et/ou cloud À l’aise dans le contact direct avec les clients À l’aise aussi bien dans le support utilisateur que dans des tâches d’ingénierie systèmes Autonome, structuré·e et responsable En veille technologique continue, avec l’envie d’apprendre Ton environnement technique Microsoft 365 (Exchange, SharePoint, Teams), Azure & Entra ID Intune et les concepts de Modern Workplace Virtualisation (Hyper-V, VMware) Sauvegarde & reprise (Veeam) Notions solides en sécurité (identité, protection des endpoints, contrôle des accès) Tu ne dois pas tout maîtriser dès le premier jour : nous privilégions l’état d’esprit, la capacité d’analyse et l’envie d’évoluer. Pourquoi rejoindre Anderson ? Un impact réel sur les clients et les solutions mises en place Des projets Cloud & Sécurité modernes Un rôle qui combine expertise technique et relation client Des perspectives d’évolution selon ton profil et tes ambitions Ce que nous offrons Package salarial compétitif Voiture de société et carte carburant Chèques-repas, éco-chèques, primes Télétravail avec indemnité forfaitaire Assurance groupe et hospitalisation (couverture familiale) GSM et abonnement Budget formation & certifications Une équipe engagée et technique

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5587116331.scraped: Who are we? Wooclap is a fast-growing edtech company on a mission to make learning more interactive and impactful . Used by millions of teachers, trainers, and students worldwide, our platform helps turn passive audiences into active participants . Our flagship product, Wooclap, is trusted by leading educational institutions such as UCLouvain, KU Leuven, Université libre de Bruxelles (ULB), HEC Paris and École Polytechnique, as well as major companies including Solvay, Deloitte, BNP Paribas Fortis, TotalEnergies, and Vinci. More recently, we launched Wooflash , a revision platform designed to complement Wooclap by supporting pedagogical continuity and personalized learning paths. With a team of 80+ passionate people, you can choose to work from our Brussels or Paris office , joining a diverse, supportive, and collaborative environment focused on building meaningful products with real-world impact . About the role In this role, you’ll join the DevX team composed of **6 team members.**You’ll work closely with the Engineering Manager – DevX, and the broader engineering teams. You’ll play a key role in improving developer productivity, delivery reliability, platform resilience and security at scale. You’ll work at the intersection of people, product and platform , with a strong focus on impact, pragmatism and long-term sustainability. You’ll contribute to the foundations of our engineering platform: CI/CD, infrastructure, deployment, observability and security , with a strong “security & reliability by design” mindset. Your mission is simple and demanding: make the right thing the easy thing for engineers . Your responsibilitiesCI/CD & Delivery Excellence Own and continuously improve our CI/CD pipelines . Identify and deliver quick wins to reduce build times, flaky pipelines and manual steps. Improve deployment safety, speed and rollback strategies. Standardize best practices across teams while keeping pragmatism. Infrastructure & Platform Actively contribute to Infrastructure as Code (IaC) . Gradually take ownership of parts of our Terraform stack. Improve infrastructure scalability, maintainability and clarity. Collaborate closely with platform and backend engineers. Kubernetes & Deployment Use Kubernetes as a core lever to improve deployment strategies. Improve environment consistency and reliability. Help teams better understand and leverage Kubernetes without increasing cognitive load. Observability & Monitoring Build and improve monitoring, alerting and observability . Reduce noise, improve signal quality. Help teams understand system behavior, performance and incidents. Contribute to a culture of learning from incidents (not firefighting). Security Engineering (by design) Integrate security by design into CI/CD, infrastructure and workflows. Implement automated security checks and guardrails. Help define and enforce non-negotiable security standards. Actively contribute to vulnerability detection and remediation workflows. Collaboration & Impact Work in a transversal role , supporting all engineering teams. Stay close to real developer pain points. Communicate clearly on changes, risks and improvements. Favor impact over perfection. Our Tech Stack Frontend : JavaScript, React, internal design system Backend : JavaScript, Node.js, MongoDB Platform : AWS, Terraform, GitHub Actions We work in continuous integration and ship to production frequently, with a strong focus on reliability and security.
