https://www.adzuna.be/details/5576255459.scraped: About Lizy LIZY is revolutionizing the €70 billion European car leasing industry, one of the last giants yet to be disrupted. Traditional leasing is slow, confusing, and overpriced. We’re changing that. At LIZY, we believe leasing a car should be as seamless as buying a toaster on Amazon. With the premium touch of a Four Seasons experience. Since launching in 2019, we've been on an unstoppable growth journey, driven by bold ambition and relentless innovation. Our vision? To become the #1 digital car leasing platform in Europe. Customers love us: over 250 raving reviews and an average rating of 4.7 stars. Headquartered in Brussels, we’ve already expanded into Paris and Amsterdam, and we’re just getting started. Backed by over €40 million in funding and powered by a dream team of top talent from Google, McKinsey, Bain, Deloitte, Cowboy, Teamleader, and more, we’re building the future of car leasing. LIZY values Fun (Have it): Enjoy the ride - life is short. Feedback (Give it): Feedback is the breakfast of champions. Always assume positive intent. Personal Growth (Master it): Never stop learning and improving. Wow (Deliver it): Actually make our customers say "WOW". ️‍♂️ Drive (Show it): Show commitment and bring everything you have. Do what you say and say what you do The Position We are looking for a Business Automation Engineer to act as the "force multiplier" for LIZY. You will sit at the intersection of Business, Operations, and Technology, using the latest no-code and AI tools to turn manual, complex processes into automated magic. You won't just be a ticket-taker, you are a builder at heart with a strong business mind. You will follow the product roadmap to deploy robust internal tools, while simultaneously acting as the "go-to expert" for business teams who are building their own smaller automations. This is a role for a tech-savvy problem solver who loves to hack processes, is obsessed with the latest AI trends, and gets a kick out of saving the company hundreds of hours of manual work. What you’ll be doing: Build & deploy : You are the primary builder for complex, cross-functional automations. You will use tools like Make, n8n, Zapier, and Python scripting to connect our systems and automate heavy operational workflows. Process architecture : Before building, you analyze. You work with business teams to deconstruct their pain points, challenge their logic, and structure data flows that make sense for the business. You’ll have the chance to interact with all functions at LIZY, having a transversal role with major impact for the organisation. Coach & unblock : You are the internal champion for automation. When Sales or Ops colleagues get stuck building their own automations, you are the expert they call for help. You review their logic and help them level up. Tech watch & innovation : The field of AI and Automation moves fast. You are responsible for staying curious, testing new tools, and bringing fresh ideas to the Product team to keep our no-code stack cutting-edge. Contribute to the roadmap : You work closely with Product to identify opportunities. You don’t just build what is asked; you provide feedback on feasibility and suggest smarter ways to achieve the goal. ‍‍♂️About you You are a builder first. You love getting your hands dirty. You prefer prototyping and shipping a working solution over writing a 10-page strategy document. You speak business and tech. You can explain API constraints to a salesperson, and you can explain a sales funnel to a developer. You have a strong business acumen and are able to empathise with different areas of an organization. You are endlessly curious. You are the person who reads about a new AI model release and immediately tries to figure out how to use it to streamline tedious repetitive tasks. You know that a bad process automated is just a faster bad process. You care about error handling, documentation, and maintainability. Your profile: 1-3 years in a role blending Ops and Tech (e.g., Business analyst, Tech consultant, Operations manager with a coding background). Experience building automation flows in Make, Zapier, n8n (or similar) is essential. Functional knowledge of Python (or JavaScript) to manipulate data and connect APIs when no-code isn't enough is a plus. You understand how databases relate (SQL concepts) and how to structure data cleanly. You have a "Process Improvement" brain. You look at a manual task and instinctively calculate how to automate it. Fluent in English. Perks and Benefits At LIZY, we know that building a winning business starts with building an incredible team. That’s why we go the extra mile to make sure our people are supported, empowered, and inspired—every single day. Here’s what you can expect when you join us: Competitive & Optimized Package: We offer an attractive compensation package that includes a well-structured salary and a lot of other benefits. Flexible Mobility Budget: Need a car? Great. Prefer other mobility options? We’ve got you covered. You choose what fits your lifestyle best. Cool Offices in the Heart of Brussels: Expect a vibrant atmosphere, awesome colleagues, specialty coffee, premium tea, and lunch paid by the company every Wednesday. ☀️ Flexible Work, Your Way: You can work remotely up to 2-3 days a week. Top-Tier Tools: We equip you with the best: high-end laptop, external monitor, iPhone, and anything else you need to do your best work. Stock Options: When LIZY wins, so do you. We believe in sharing success. A Culture That Actually Cares: Expect positivity, transparency, and support—because we believe great things happen when people feel good at work. We care about each other and we show it—through weekly team lunches, monthly All Hands meetings with cake and drinks, and fun team-building events throughout the year.

active:bg-adzuna-green-700 text-center block m-4 md:mx-0 md:my-4 md:inline-block " Solliciteren /a section class="adp-body mx-4 mb-4 text-sm md:mx-0 md:text-base md:mb-0" Actemium Belgium conseille, conçoit, réalise et soutient des processus industriels. Nous offrons également du service et des formations. Nous sommes actifs en électricité et instrumentation, en informatique / TIC, en automatisation et en mécanique et tuyauterie. Notre mission est d'aider nos clients à améliorer leur avantage concurrentiel ainsi que leur performance industrielle. Actemium Belgium emploie aujourd'hui 900 personnes, réparties dans 8 sites en Belgique, 1 en Chine et 1 en France. Actemium est une marque de VINCI Energies. Actemium, marque de VINCI Energies Belgium, entièrement dédiée à l'Industrie est aussi leader dans la fourniture de solutions et de services à l'Industrie. Notre focus, l'amélioration des processus industriels chez nos clients. Dans le cadre de notre croissance en Wallonie, nous recherchons un Automation Engineer avec une solide expérience en processus industriels et un esprit d'équipe fort. Au sein d'une équipe projets, vous travaillez de manière autonome à des solutions sur mesure chez nos clients actifs en Wallonie et issus exclusivement du secteur industriel. Responsabiités Programmer et implémenter des systèmes PLC, SCADA ou DCS chez nos clients Intégrer et paramétrer des systèmes IT industriels Analyser les process et réfléchir, le cas échéant, à la mise au point de nouveaux concepts Réaliser des analyses fonctionnelles adaptées aux situations techniques rencontrées Implémenter les fonctionnalités et réaliser les mises en service chez nos clients Rédiger la documentation technique de la solution Supporter/exécuter les processus de validation du système Profil Master en Sciences de l'Ingénieur Industriel, Master/Bachelor Automation Idéalement 5 ans d'expérience en automatisation industrielle, une expérience dans l'industrie de process constitue un réel atout Connaissance en automates programmables Siemens (S7, TIA, PCS7) Connaissance en systèmes de supervision SCADA (WinCC, InTouch, Aveva System Platform) constitue un sérieux atout Esprit analytoque fort et rigueur (respect des délais et des normes de sécurité) Flexible et curieux, vous adoptez une attitude « problem-solving » en toute circonstance Francophone, vous êtes à l'aise en anglais Notre offre Actemium fait partie du groupe VINCI Energies. Chez nous, l'accent est mis sur : Des projets innovants L'Esprit d'équipe, l'implication dans l'organisation et le développement personnel Une formation sur mesure pour encadrer de manière optimale vos débuts chez nous Un parcours de carrière clair ... Actemium Belgium emploie aujourd'hui 900 personnes, réparties dans 8 sites en Belgique, 1 en Chine et 1 en France. Actemium est une marque de VINCI Energies. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5619875118.scraped: Project Overview The primary goal of this internship is to transform our internal documentation into a high-functioning, visually engaging knowledge hub. Using a pre-defined structural proposition, you will lead the migration of existing knowledge, including the Atrias Academy , into Confluence. This isn't just about "copy-pasting"; it’s about creating a seamless user experience for every employee who needs to find information. Key Responsibilities System Implementation: Execute the migration of paper-based structures into a live, organized Confluence environment. Content Transformation: Review legacy documentation and rewrite/reformat it to ensure it is concise, accurate, and easy to navigate. Visual Design: Utilize Confluence macros, layouts, and visual elements to make pages "scannable" and aesthetically pleasing. Academy Integration: Transition the Atrias Academy materials into the new structure, ensuring learning paths are intuitive for new and existing staff. Quality Assurance: Audit links, permissions, and page hierarchies to ensure the "new world" of our documentation is bug-free and logically sound. Required Skills & Qualifications Category Desired Skillset Technical Familiarity with Confluence (or similar wikis/CMS) and an understanding of Information Architecture . Communication Strong technical writing skills; the ability to take complex info and make it "human-readable." Design Eye A knack for UI/UX, knowing how to use white space, headings, and imagery to prevent "walls of text." Mindset High attention to detail and a structured, methodical approach to problem-solving. What’s in it for the Intern? Ownership: They won't just be a "task-runner"; they will be responsible for a tangible, company-wide tool. Portfolio Building: They can demonstrate a clear "Before vs. After" transformation of a corporate knowledge base. Strategic Impact: Their work will directly improve the onboarding speed and daily efficiency of the entire team. About Accenture Accenture is a leading global professional services company that helps the world’s leading businesses, governments and other organizations build their digital core, optimize their operations, accelerate revenue growth and enhance citizen services—creating tangible value at speed and scale. We are a talent- and innovation-led company with approximately 791,000 people serving clients in more than 120 countries. Technology is at the core of change today, and we are one of the world’s leaders in helping drive that change, with strong ecosystem relationships. We combine our strength in technology and leadership in cloud, data and AI with unmatched industry experience, functional expertise and global delivery capability. Our broad range of services, solutions and assets across Strategy & Consulting, Technology, Operations, Industry X and Song, together with our culture of shared success and commitment to creating 360° value, enable us to help our clients reinvent and build trusted, lasting relationships. We measure our success by the 360° value we create for our clients, each other, our shareholders, partners and communities. Visit us at www.accenture.com Equal Employment Opportunity Statement We believe that no one should be discriminated against because of their differences. All employment decisions shall be made without regard to age, race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, disability status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, citizenship status or any other basis as protected by applicable law. Our rich diversity makes us more innovative, more competitive, and more creative, which helps us better serve our clients and our communities.

Technology is at the core of change today, and we are one of the world’s leaders in helping drive that change, with strong ecosystem relationships. We combine our strength in technology and leadership in cloud, data and AI with unmatched industry experience, functional expertise and global delivery capability. https://www.adzuna.be/details/5630323956.scraped: Joining Collibra’s Cloud Operations Team We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Production Release Engineer to join our dynamic team. In this pivotal role, you will be instrumental in ensuring the seamless, reliable, and efficient delivery of our cutting-edge cloud services. You will act as a bridge between development, operations, and business stakeholders, driving continuous improvement, fostering innovation, and actively contributing to the architectural integrity and scalability of our platforms. This position is remote. DevOps Engineers at Collibra are responsible for Proactively research and integrate emerging technologies and cutting-edge software practices into our release processes and infrastructure. Formulate and champion new ideas and features to continuously enhance our infrastructure, functionality, and overall product effectiveness. Apply data modeling and predictive analysis techniques to anticipate and mitigate potential issues before they impact production. Act as a key liaison and facilitator between Production Engineering, other technical functions, external vendors, business partners, and end-users. Lead and actively participate in cross-team engineering discussions to design and evolve scalable, measurable, fault-tolerant, and cost-effective cloud services. Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure a holistic approach to identifying, addressing, and resolving platform and functionality issues. Actively participate in architectural design reviews and discussions, providing valuable input on features, service improvements, and new service implementations. Utilize visual tools, such as flowcharts and diagrams, effectively during design and problem-solving processes. Provide high-level support and expertise for team and cross-functional projects related to release automation and infrastructure. Maintain clear and complete understanding of complex situations through intent listening and incisive questioning to ensure accurate interpretation and resolution. You Have 5 years of experience in Release Engineering, DevOps, or a similar role focused on software delivery and infrastructure. Proven track record of driving process improvements and implementing automation in complex environments. Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) and their respective services. Strong proficiency in CI/CD pipelines and tools (e.g., Jenkins, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps, ArgoCD). Expertise in scripting and automation languages (e.g., Python, Go, Bash). Familiarity with containerization (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes). Understanding of infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) principles and tools (e.g., Terraform, CloudFormation). Experience with monitoring, logging, and observability tools. Professional experience installing, configuring, managing, and/or administering ElasticSearch. You Are Exceptional with communication and interpersonal skills and have the ability to articulate complex technical concepts clearly to diverse audiences. Strong in analytical and problem-solving abilities, with a proactive and preventative mindset. Able to influence and lead discussions across engineering teams. Self-driven and takes initiative to identify and implement improvements. Measures of Success Within your first month, you will gain a deep understanding of existing release pipelines and infrastructure, proactively identifying initial pain points and potential areas for improvement. Within your third month, you will establish strong working relationships and trust with key stakeholders across development, product, and operations teams. Within your sixth month, you will contribute to a quantifiable improvement in at least one key DORA metric (e.g., Change Failure Rate reduction, Deployment Lead Time improvement) within a critical release pipeline. You will successfully implement or significantly contribute to new automation efforts that demonstrably streamline a specific aspect of the release process.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5608563256.scraped: DevSecOps Engineer For my International client I am seeking a DevSecOps Engineer to join the team on a contract assignment ( initially for 80 days but renewable ). This is hybrid role with mainly off-site working but up to 5 days per month on site in Brussels. Tasks and Responsibilities of the DevSecOps Engineer Plan, implement, and upgrade DevSecOps pipelines. Facilitate continuous delivery of IT applications using Application Lifecycle Management and automation tools. Promote the adoption of DevSecOps methodologies across IT projects. Architect and design solutions for API Security, Container Security, and Cloud Security. Collaborate with business analysts, customers, users, project leaders, and developers. The skills and experience we seek for this role Master's degree in IT with minimum 15 years of relevant experience (or Bachelor's degree and minimum of 19 years relevant experience). Minimum 3 years of experience with DevOps container or serverless/orchestration tools (e.g., Docker, Ansible, Terraform, buildpacks, Kubernetes). Strong knowledge in establishing and managing deployment pipelines and release management (e.g., Bamboo, Puppet). Proficiency in DevOps container or serverless/orchestration tools (e.g., Docker, Ansible, Terraform, buildpacks, Kubernetes). Solid experience with JAVA and Angular development. Good understanding of black-box and white-box testing (e.g., OWASP, HP Fortify). Familiarity with monitoring tools (e.g., Dynatrace, Prometheus). Cloud experience (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, OVH). Experience conducting DevSecOps in an Agile work environment. Certified DevSecOps Professional (CDP), AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional, Microsoft DevOps Engineer Expert, Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer, or equivalent certification is a plus. Fluent in English at least at a level B2. French will be considered an advantage

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5628780919.scraped: Cloud engineering Service We ARE Renewables is looking for a Cloud Engineer for one of our clients in the energy sector. Task and Responsibilities Cloud Infrastructure Management Operate, monitor, and troubleshoot managed and self-hosted platforms on Azure, including Kubernetes, Kafka, PostgreSQL, Keycloak, and Opensearch Apply patching and routine updates in line with organizational and security processes. Configure and manage access controls and secrets (e.g., with Keycloak and platform-native tools) Maintain configuration management (e.g., with tools like Terraform, Helm) Produce and update technical and operational documentation for assigned infrastructure components DevOps & Automation Support deployment activities and maintain CI/CD pipelines, leveraging Azure DevOps or similar tools Monitoring & Optimization Monitor system health and respond to platform incidents, escalating complex issues to senior staff or specialists when required Provide input to capacity and performance monitoring, reporting detected bottlenecks or improvement opportunities Support & maintenance Perform routine backups, restore operations, and basic disaster recovery activities as per documented processes Collaborate with development/engineering, security, and other teams to resolve system-level issues and support platform changes Collaboration & Support Work closely with the team to develop and deploy cloud components and infrastructure as defined in the product backlog and user stories, ensuring technical tasks are completed efficiently and to quality standards Take part in Scrum ceremonies such as sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives to share progress, address obstacles, and align work with team goals Work collaboratively with team members, contribute to ongoing refinement of processes and solutions based on agile feedback, and assist with troubleshooting and optimizing cloud services throughout the iterative development cycle Key Requirements Hands-on experience in cloud infrastructure operations with Azure, including resource provisioning, monitoring, and cost awareness Azure Access Control Azure Virtual Network Logic App Azure Firewall Azure VPN Gateway Azure Monitoring Operational skills in deploying, configuring, and supporting Kubernetes, Kafka, PostgreSQL, CEPH, Opensearch, Authentication and Authorization incl. Keycloak Familiarity with infrastructure as code and container orchestration tools (Terraform) Basic scripting and automation (e.g., PowerShell, Bash, Python) for operational tasks Experience working with CI/CD pipelines and version control (e.g., Azure DevOps, Git, ArgoCD, Helm) Solid understanding of cloud security, identity and access management, and data protection Location: Brussels + Hybrid Duration: March 2026 – December 2026

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5588904814.scraped: Toyota Motor Europe offers an 18-month Graduate Program in warehouse operations at its Parts Centre in Diest, Belgium. The key task first is to join a project called warehouse reborn (very large transformation project to improve Safety, Ergonomics, Quality, Delivery, Efficiency & Members). Also, the role combines training and hands-on experience, leading to a supervisor position managing a team of around 30 members. Ideal candidates have a background in engineering, logistics, or automotive, are fluent in Dutch and English, and show strong leadership and problem-solving skills. TOYOTA is one of the world’s largest automobile manufacturers and a leading global corporation. Founded in 1937. Toyota now sells vehicles in 170 countries and employs over 350.000 people. Based in Brussels, Belgium, and staffed by 2.700 people and more than 60 nationalities, Toyota Motor Europe (TME) handles the wholesale marketing of Toyota and Lexus vehicles, parts & accessories, and manages Toyota’s European R&D, manufacturing, and engineering operations. We are excited to be transforming into a mobility company focusing on bringing even more happiness to our customers through various mobility solutions. Team description Parts Supply Chain Group mission is to deliver the right product (service parts, accessories and information) at the right time, through lean integrated logistics, exceeding Customer & Stakeholder expectation, with engaged members realising a sustainable future. Warehouse Reborn is a large-scale transformation project where the whole warehouse operation is transformed to support future business growth and challenges. The focus points are to increase our members safety, ergonomics, as well as the efficiency of the flows and the density of the storage thanks to innovative ideas and products. You will first join Warehouse Reborn project (18 months minimum) before transferring into Warehouse Supervisor role (shift pattern). Your responsibilities Warehouse Reborn project leader: Think ahead: develop the strategy (goals, stakeholders, planning…) Convince and collaborate to reach consensus Implement the project with ownership Follow up and communicate your deliverables Reflect on the learnings and share with the organisation Warehouse supervisor: Lead and develop 4 team leaders and 32 members Performance reporting Ensure your area has the right resources to perform the tasks Implementation and follow up standardised work Solve problem with Toyota methodology For TME Graduate positions we are targeting recent University Graduates (less than 18 months of experience excluding internships) You are fluent in English (TME’s business language) and dutch You are a career starter (less than 18 months of experience excluding internships) You have a bachelor / master degree in supply chain, logistics, engineering Your values are in line with the Toyota way You have experience in project management You are analytical and can visualise data You are structured, logical and rational You are a team player with excellent communication & collaboration skills You take initiative and ownership You want to lead and manage a team in an operational environment You have a desire to learn, grow and develop your skills & competences You master Excel, Powerpoint, and other Microsoft 365 tools We offer a dynamic multicultural work environment with broad opportunities to learn every day and exciting career pathways that help you explore different disciplines or areas of expertise. Your benefits: Permanent contract and a structured 18-month Graduate Development Programme Competitive salary package based on your experience, including a bonus plan depending on individual and/or company results; Flexible reward plan that will allow you to personalise part of your reward plan and balance it in line with your priorities by opting for extra insurance, more mobility options (bike lease), extra holidays and more; Meal and eco vouchers; Insurance package (medical, life, disability, pension); Extra paid holidays (Toyota days, seniority days…) above the legal holidays. More can be added through our flexible reward package; Flexible working hours and hybrid working (homeworking, 20 days from abroad); Interesting car lease formulas and discount when buying a Toyota or Lexus; Various company discounts – Exceptional terms and prices on products and services from your favourite brands and retailers; Company restaurant – Warm dishes, salads and desserts daily, including Japanese and traditional Belgian specialties, as well as a vegan/vegetarian option; Sport facilities – Tennis, squash, volleyball, football and softball pitches are available at various Toyota sites; Team building activities and other personnel related events; Programs to support your physical and social well-being. At Toyota Motor Europe, we are committed to providing equal employment opportunity to all. All our recruitment decisions are based on the individual and their qualifications along with the job requirements. The selection process is without regard to nationality, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, religion, ethnicity, disability or other personal characteristics. We do not tolerate harassment or discrimination as our individual experiences and overall diversity is our key strength. Together we have the power to make a positive change and go beyond the ordinary. If you need any specific consideration for the interview process due to disability or any other reason, please let the recruiter know.

Team description Parts Supply Chain Group mission is to deliver the right product (service parts, accessories and information) at the right time, through lean integrated logistics, exceeding Customer & Stakeholder expectation, with engaged members realising a sustainable future. Warehouse Reborn is a large-scale transformation project where the whole warehouse operation is transformed to support future business growth and challenges. The focus points are to increase our members safety, ergonomics, as well as the efficiency of the flows and the density of the storage thanks to innovative ideas and products. You will first join Warehouse Reborn project (18 months minimum) before transferring into Warehouse Supervisor role (shift pattern). Your responsibilities Warehouse Reborn project leader: Think ahead: develop the strategy (goals, stakeholders, planning…) Convince and collaborate to reach consensus Implement the project with ownership Follow up and communicate your deliverables Reflect on the learnings and share with the organisation Warehouse supervisor: Lead and develop 4 team leaders and 32 members Performance reporting Ensure your area has the right resources to perform the tasks Implementation and follow up standardised work Solve problem with Toyota methodology For TME Graduate positions we are targeting recent University Graduates (less than 18 months of experience excluding internships) You are fluent in English (TME’s business language) and dutch You are a career starter (less than 18 months of experience excluding internships) You have a bachelor / master degree in supply chain, logistics, engineering Your values are in line with the Toyota way You have experience in project management You are analytical and can visualise data You are structured, logical and rational You are a team player with excellent communication & collaboration skills You take initiative and ownership You want to lead and manage a team in an operational environment You have a desire to learn, grow and develop your skills & competences You master Excel, Powerpoint, and other Microsoft 365 tools We offer a dynamic multicultural work environment with broad opportunities to learn every day and exciting career pathways that help you explore different disciplines or areas of expertise. Your benefits: Permanent contract and a structured 18-month Graduate Development Programme Competitive salary package based on your experience, including a bonus plan depending on individual and/or company results; Flexible reward plan that will allow you to personalise part of your reward plan and balance it in line with your priorities by opting for extra insurance, more mobility options (bike lease), extra holidays and more; Meal and eco vouchers; Insurance package (medical, life, disability, pension); Extra paid holidays (Toyota days, seniority days…) above the legal holidays. More can be added through our flexible reward package; Flexible working hours and hybrid working (homeworking, 20 days from abroad); Interesting car lease formulas and discount when buying a Toyota or Lexus; Various company discounts – Exceptional terms and prices on products and services from your favourite brands and retailers; Company restaurant – Warm dishes, salads and desserts daily, including Japanese and traditional Belgian specialties, as well as a vegan/vegetarian option; Sport facilities – Tennis, squash, volleyball, football and softball pitches are available at various Toyota sites; Team building activities and other personnel related events; Programs to support your physical and social well-being. At Toyota Motor Europe, we are committed to providing equal employment opportunity to all. All our recruitment decisions are based on the individual and their qualifications along with the job requirements. The selection process is without regard to nationality, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, religion, ethnicity, disability or other personal characteristics. We do not tolerate harassment or discrimination as our individual experiences and overall diversity is our key strength. Together we have the power to make a positive change and go beyond the ordinary. If you need any specific consideration for the interview process due to disability or any other reason, please let the recruiter know.

Proficiency in scripting languages (Python, Bash). Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP). Strong knowledge of version control systems (Git). Understanding of networking fundamentals and security principles. Soft Skills Excellent collaboration and communication abilities. Ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams. Strong problem-solving mindset and proactive attitude. High commitment to quality, reliability, and security. Adaptability and resilience in a fast-paced environment. Languages English: Professional proficiency (required). Dutch or French: Considered an asset.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5619869783.scraped: Building Automation Engineer Are you a hands-on problem solver with a passion for making buildings smarter, greener and more efficient? This is your chance to join a forward-thinking company leading the way in building automation and energy optimisation across Belgium. Clear are looking for a Building Automation Engineer who excels in managing complex projects, solving technical challenges, and ensuring successful delivery. You’ll be responsible for leading the design, coordination, and implementation of advanced automation systems in commercial buildings, hospitals, data centres, and other high-profile facilities, ensuring projects are completed on time, within scope, and to the highest standards. Flemish Region €4,000 - €5,500 Monthly Salary 13 Months Salary Lunch Tokens Bonus Car or Car Allowance Overtime Expenses Training and Development Great Social Environment Responsibilities: Designing and engineering building automation and control system solutions to meet project specifications. Developing system architectures, control strategies and detailed engineering documentation. Programming and configuring controllers, HMIs, and communication protocols (e.g., BACnet, Modbus, KNX). Overseeing factory acceptance testing (FAT), site installation, commissioning and system integration. Collaborating closely with multidisciplinary teams to deliver reliable and optimised automation solutions. Desired Experience: Proven experience in building automation, BMS, HVAC controls or similar. Strong troubleshooting skills and an eye for detail. Comfortable working both independently and as part of a collaborative team. Norwegian and English language skills (both ideal). What's on Offer: Competitive salary and benefits package. Opportunities to work on diverse, high-impact projects across Oslo and beyond. Access to the latest tools and technologies in building automation. A supportive team that values your expertise and helps you grow further. INDEU

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5571012983.scraped: (H/F) DevSecOps Engineer As a DevSecOps Engineer , your role is to: Set up and monitor all the steps, tools, and practices that enable the development, testing, and deployment of software applications at Speos, in order to improve the performance of every stage of the delivery chain. Evolve CI/CD and operational platforms by integrating best practices in automation, observability, and security. You act as a facilitator for developers, as a guarantor of quality, and as a key player in the company’s technical transformation. Your responsibilities As a DevSecOps Engineer , your responsibilities include: CI/CD Platform – Design, Evolution, and Maintenance Design, maintain, and evolve the CI/CD platform to provide developers with a modern, intuitive, and productive tool. Integrate leading technologies (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, ArgoCD…) to optimize pipeline efficiency. Measure and improve pipeline performance (execution time, success/failure rate) to ensure robustness. Deploy Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to version pipelines and environments. Security by Design – Continuous Security Integration (DevSecOps) Maintain and evolve vulnerability scanning tools (source code, libraries and dependencies, Docker images). Implement automated security policies in pipelines to detect vulnerabilities early in the development process. Integrate secret and credential verification in the CI/CD process. Ensure secure secrets management (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager…). Software Quality – Test Automation Ensure the automation of unit tests in CI pipelines. Promote integration and automated security testing. Measure and report test coverage to developers. Automate static and dynamic code analysis (linting, SAST, etc.). Artifact and Registry Management Administer code repositories (GitLab), library repositories (Nexus), and image registries (Nexus) to ensure artifact availability. Implement retention, tagging, cleanup, and security policies to guarantee integrity and version control. Deployment Automation (CD) Build and maintain automated deployment pipelines to accelerate production release cycles. Integrate security or approval gates into production flows to secure releases. Ensure consistency and stability across environments (Dev, Test, Prod). Track and trace each deployment (logs, signatures, audits). Observability, Monitoring, and Performance Deploy and maintain observability tools (logs, traces, metrics) to ensure system visibility. Ensure application and infrastructure monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK…). Optimize resource usage and detect anomalies. Provide meaningful alerts and facilitate incident diagnosis. Compliance, Audit, and Governance Maintain CI/CD action and access traceability to meet security and audit requirements. Contribute to the preparation of security or quality audits (ISO 27001, GDPR…). Document processes, policies, and security rules to support compliance and transparency. Participate in compliance reviews and risk management. DevSecOps Culture and Team Enablement Support developers in using CI/CD tools and best practices. Lead training sessions or workshops on security awareness (OWASP, secure coding…). Promote a DevSecOps culture based on automation, collaboration, and feedback. Contribute to incident resolution and continuous improvement post-mortems. Continuous Improvement of the Operational Platform Identify business needs and adapt infrastructure accordingly. Drive structural technical evolutions (containers, Kubernetes, GitOps…). Ensure scalability, resilience, and availability of the platform. Proactively suggest relevant emerging technologies for Speos. Your profile As a DevSecOps Engineer , you bring: Minimum 2 years of experience as a DevOps Engineer. Strong knowledge of application security principles and pipelines (DAST, SAST, secret management). Expertise in CI/CD and automation of development workflows. Proficiency in Infrastructure as Code (Terraform). Knowledge of public cloud (AWS). Familiarity with observability and alerting tools (ELK, Grafana). Strong communication and cross-team collaboration skills. Fluency in French and English is a must (Dutch is a plus).

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5583162512.scraped: Description This position is part of the FN BBN TOP DevOps team, which defines and implements the digital architecture for key R&D areas in support of a business transformation to achieve product development acceleration/continuous delivery on the longer term. The DevOps Continuous Integration Engineer contributes to the FN BBN DevOps enablement roadmap implementation following the priorities set by the DevOps transformation lead. He or she will be responsible for optimizing the sw integration & test flows within FN BBN. Cooperation with product delivery units and service teams is key part of the job. As an expert, he or she will leverage on and re-use DevOps practices and tools introduced by other BUs and explore new possibilities provided by Open Source SW. #NIFN2026 Responsibilities Own the CI/CD Pipeline: Design, implement, and manage robust and scalable CI/CD pipelines for our software development lifecycle, ensuring smooth and efficient integration and delivery. Champion Containerization and Automation: Leverage your expertise in Docker and Kubernetes to build and manage containerized applications and automate deployment processes. Master Build Systems and Scripting: Utilize your strong scripting skills (Python, Bash) and knowledge of build systems (Make, Bazel) to optimize build processes and implement advanced caching techniques. Thrive in a Linux Environment: Demonstrate your deep understanding of Linux systems, including system administration, networking, and security, to ensure the stability and performance of our CI/CD infrastructure. Collaborate and Problem-Solve: Work closely with development teams to troubleshoot issues, implement best practices, and continuously improve our CI/CD processes and tools. Qualifications Champion CI/CD Automation: Design, develop, and implement automation scripts and solutions to streamline and optimize the entire CI/CD pipeline for FN BBN products. Manage & Harden Environments: Take ownership of build and test environments, ensuring their stability, security, and scalability across physical infrastructure, OpenStack, and Kubernetes. Accelerate Software Delivery: Continuously improve CI availability and cycle times to accelerate the software validation and delivery process for increased efficiency and faster time-to-market. Elevate Code Quality & Testing: Strengthen static code analysis practices and enhance test automation frameworks to ensure high-quality code and robust software releases. Collaborate & Drive Improvements: Partner with product development, cloud infrastructure, and operations teams to identify and implement CI/CD pipeline optimizations and process improvements. Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written (EN). Cross-cultural knowledge and global mindset.

Collaborate and Problem-Solve: Work closely with development teams to troubleshoot issues, implement best practices, and continuously improve our CI/CD processes and tools. Qualifications Champion CI/CD Automation: Design, develop, and implement automation scripts and solutions to streamline and optimize the entire CI/CD pipeline for FN BBN products. Manage & Harden Environments: Take ownership of build and test environments, ensuring their stability, security, and scalability across physical infrastructure, OpenStack, and Kubernetes. Accelerate Software Delivery: Continuously improve CI availability and cycle times to accelerate the software validation and delivery process for increased efficiency and faster time-to-market. Elevate Code Quality & Testing: Strengthen static code analysis practices and enhance test automation frameworks to ensure high-quality code and robust software releases. Collaborate & Drive Improvements: Partner with product development, cloud infrastructure, and operations teams to identify and implement CI/CD pipeline optimizations and process improvements. Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written (EN). Cross-cultural knowledge and global mindset.

https://www.adzuna.be/details/5598849523.scraped: Actemium, marque de VINCI Energies Belgium, entièrement dédiée à l'Industrie est aussi leader dans la fourniture de solutions et de services à l'Industrie. Notre focus, l'amélioration des processus industriels chez nos clients. Dans le cadre de notre croissance en Wallonie, nous recherchons un Automation Engineer avec une solide expérience en processus industriels et un esprit d'équipe fort. Au sein d'une équipe projets, vous travaillez de manière autonome à des solutions sur mesure chez nos clients actifs en Wallonie et issus exclusivement du secteur industriel. Responsabilités Programmer et implémenter des systèmes PLC, SCADA ou DCS chez nos clients Intégrer et paramétrer des systèmes IT industriels Analyser les process et réfléchir, le cas échéant, à la mise au point de nouveaux concepts Réaliser des analyses fonctionnelles adaptées aux situations techniques rencontrées Implémenter les fonctionnalités et réaliser les mises en service chez nos clients Rédiger la documentation technique de la solution Supporter/exécuter les processus de validation du système Profil Master en Sciences de l’Ingénieur Industriel, Master/Bachelor Automation Idéalement 5 ans d'expérience en automatisation industrielle, une expérience dans l’industrie de process constitue un réel atout Connaissances en automates programmables Siemens (S7, TIA, PCS7) Connaissances en systèmes de supervision SCADA (WinCC, InTouch, Aveva System Platform) constitue un sérieux atout Esprit analytoque fort et rigueur (respect des délais et des normes de sécurité) Flexible et curieux, vous adoptez une attitude « problem-solving » en toute circonstance Francophone, vous êtes à l’aise en anglais Notre offre Actemium fait partie du groupe VINCI Energies. Chez nous, l’accent est mis sur : Des projets innovants L’Esprit d’équipe, l’implication dans l’organisation et le développement personnel Une formation sur mesure pour encadrer de manière optimale vos débuts chez nous Un parcours de carrière clair avec vue sur votre avenir et des perspectives d’évolution Un package salarial complet avec voiture de société, bonus, flexibilité et avantages extra-légaux De nombreuses initiatives autour du partage de connaissances, des activités sportives, familiales et culturelles ainsi que de cohésion d'équipes
